programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family prepare the farm for the arrival of two piglets, and head to Stonehenge to experience the sunrise over the ancient monument on the autumn equinox.

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: On the Ashby Canal, candlemaker Connor gears up for a day of trading and crosses paths with floating fave The Curry Cruiser. Peter and Noni continue their Scottish adventure.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Following two British families who have set up shop in Spain and are selling property to Brits looking for a bit of fun in the sun.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Penny has been a therapist, artist and teacher for over 30 years, but now wants to move to the Spanish sunshine permanently and run her business from her new home in Málaga. Laura Hamilton lines up five great options for her budget of £120,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Siblings Michael and Catherine are pooling their resources to buy a holiday home in Manilva on Spain's Costa del Sol. Jean Johansson shows them what £175k can buy.

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: With the wedding just days away, the pressure's on for Linda and Peter's stunning staircase renovations. And can Matt and Amelia find their forever family château?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Horse lovers Mel and James want a holiday home in Mijas Pueblo in southern Spain. Laura Hamilton shows them what £180k can buy. Do any of the places leave Mel and James chomping at the bit?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day one of the competition in east London. Fashionista Simran Samra hosts a glitz and glam night for her guests as she goes head to head with doctor Chintu Gademsetty, comedian Yoshki Greenberg, city boy Alan Price and businesswoman Delia Elliot. Simran cooks meat despite being a vegetarian and buys wine despite the fact she knows nothing about it, and her main course isn't a success.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day two in east London, laid-back comedian Yoshki Greenberg aims to throw a relaxing dinner party and give her guests a good laugh, but Yoshki has a surprise up her sleeve: on arrival she hands her guests animal-themed pyjama suits. Sadly not all the guests are put at ease by wearing somebody else's clothes, especially as they haven't even been washed.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day three in east London and jack-the-lad Alan Price aims to wow his guests with a medieval themed feast. After two nights of disappointing food, Alan hopes he can raise the standard this week with an ambitious menu, but his dinner party doesn't get off to the best start when his medieval table decoration, a whole pig's head, sends vegetarian Simran in to a state of panic.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day four in east London it's the turn of bar owner Delia Elliot to host. Cordon Bleu cook Delia has purposefully played down her menu in an attempt to wow her guests. Despite this, expectations are high. As the elder of the group, Delia's confident she can teach her young diners a thing or two about how to throw a good dinner party. However not all the youngsters want to listen.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in east London doctor Chintu Gademsetty is confident of winning with his 80s-themed dinner party, but first he needs to create a cheery atmosphere following the tensions of the past two nights, and secondly he needs to out-do Delia's Cordon Bleu cookery. The night begins well when Chintu delights with his MC Hammer costume and Knight Rider car, but who will be the ultimate winner?

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A new series from Woodgreen Pets Charity and more heart-warming tales of lost dog meets new owner. Can glam Poodle-cross Lola live up to the expectations of five-year-old Violet?

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: In Worsley, George helps Katherine and Andrew to renovate their awkward abode and reveal its stunning view, while landscaper Luke tackles the steep, hazardous garden.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Young Henry has been accidentally hit by a forklift truck driven by his dad. Best mates Dean and Nahum are reeling from a car crash. And 94-year-old Pearl has badly cut her arm by trapping it in a lift.

programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: Paratrooper Lee has catastrophic spine, pelvis and leg injuries requiring high-risk surgery using bone hooks. And a plasterer is put in a coma after a head-on traffic collision.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britain's best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The celebs hit the sofa for a new series. What do Rylan and his mum, Tom Allen, Mo Gilligan, Vicky Pattison, Denise van Outen, and many others, think of this week's TV?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two 19-year-olds take their first steps into the dating world together. A hip-hop artist meets another musician. And can weightlifter Jack give bodybuilder Eloise a lift?

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Duncan attends a midnight stand-off between armed officers and a man who has been seen carrying knives, Suggs pulls over a motorist who is incapable of telling the truth, and Charlie is given the unenviable task of Range Rover removal after a crash.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors are in pursuit of a road works wrecker, a 17-year-old robbery suspect, and a drink driver who does not understand the word `blow'. Dan is forced into a daylight pursuit of a Ford Focus with a shocking outcome.

programme.title: Vera Cruz
programme.desc: Two trigger-happy cowboy mercenaries head for Mexico during the 1866 revolution. A pair of beautiful women try to persuade them to fight for rival armies, but they eventually side with the Emperor Maximilian, tempted by the consignment of gold that they have been hired to escort. Inevitably, revolutionary fervour and double-dealing point the way to a violent confrontation.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A case of drunk-driving leads to a hot pursuit on the city streets and carnage on the country roads, and what starts out as a routine stop for speeding results in a lone female copper getting a worrying surprise.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A high-speed pursuit takes the officers off-road, a routine stop provides the interceptors with some tasty results and a tracker activation turns into a major haul. Elsewhere, Natalie Pinkham joins the police dog unit to find hidden drugs.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders

programme.desc: An emergency is declared when public disorder erupts in Cheltenham's Eastgate Street club district. Fights are breaking out all over the area, and the police are attacked as they attempt to restore order.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A suspect accused of discharging a firearm is caught on a bus. A man caught with a stolen Kia claims the 'real driver' fled the scene. A man claims he punched a wall instead of his girlfriend.

programme.title: The X-Files

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Water Drillers Anthony and Danyelle, enlist a new team member as family life on the road tests their marriage. Slick's cross-country race home for Christmas may be in jeopardy.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Vehicles in need of recovery include a Land Rover Discovery dumped in a canal, a lorry that has lost traction on a steep gradient, and a truck that's nosedived into a domestic back garden.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Just outside Newcastle, Kev is sent to rescue a wheelie bin delivery truck that has got stuck. With torrential rain forecast, this job is turning into a recovery nightmare.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers pull over a high-performance vehicle after reports of the driver live streaming at speeds of over 170 miles an hour. And a driver crashes into a lorry after drinking heavily.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following a tip-off about a suspected disqualified driver, officers search for and arrest the driver of a van who, when questioned, says he has no recollection of being disqualified.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Derbyshire's Road Crime Team hunt down organised crime gangs stealing cars. Following a spike in car thefts, officers are on the lookout for cross-border gangs stealing high end cars.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Justin and Paul chase down a gang of suspected car thieves. It takes seven interceptors to stop a violent thief who tries to sink his teeth into them. A drug raid turns life-threatening, and dog-handler Jimmy tries to make sense of a bloke with a blooded face.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Special episode featuring the dog squad, revealing how our canine friends are trained and prepared for tracking down criminals.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Paul and Suart are on the trail of a man suspected of stabbing his wife, Andy has a high-speed chase through a housing estate and Kev pays an early-morning visit to a drug dealer.

programme.title: Call The Bailiffs: Time To Pay Up
programme.desc: Following the work of enforcement agents as they execute High Court writs on behalf of people desperate to recover the money they are owed. When defendants are tracked down, agents face tense confrontations and dilemmas.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John hands his gym responsibilities back over to an unimpressed Lacey and arrives to help Sonny up the stairs.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Masterfully concealing his physical struggles, a determined Sonny heads for the Surf Club, aided by crutches and with Dana at his side.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the every day lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals can't.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A busy Monday becomes a record-breaker as the team deal with 349 patients. The emergency red phone ringsto alert the team that a critically ill patient is due to arrive by ambulance. Sister Benita Wainwright is co-ordinating the department.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: Matthew is rushed into hospital after a collision between his motorbike and a tractor. His chest has taken the full force of the impact but there could be other, hidden injuries.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A woman is rushed in after her motorbike collided with a deer. Strongman Adam is devastated when an accident in the gym means he may not be able to compete in Hull's strongest man.

programme.title: The Surgeon
programme.desc: Following the work of consultant breast surgeon Sascha Dua as she runs a busy clinic offering breast cosmetic work and cancer treatment while juggling parenthood as a single mum.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Paramedics help care home manager Kirsty, who fell on her arm. The team try to stabilise 67-year-old Bridget, whose heart is beating twice as fast as it should be.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The medics explore the theory that a `broken heart' can cause patients to suffer very real mental and physical problems. Dr Wyne chats to Adolphus, who is struggling with the recent death of his wife and best friend within a few weeks of each other.

programme.title: Accused: Guilty or Innocent?
programme.desc: When a Marine Corps veteran gets drawn into a domestic shoot out in El Paso, a 16-year-old boy is shot dead. Was the vet overzealous or acting in self-defence?

programme.desc: Oliver learns when he needs to wash his hands: before eating, after going to the potty, and after playing with his good friend Lola.

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes
programme.desc: Sing along with classics `I'm a little teapot', `Baa Baa Black sheep' and `Itsy Bitsy Spider'.

programme.title: Olly

programme.title: Happy Birthday to You

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes

programme.title: Bath Tubbies

programme.desc: When we meet somebody everywhere we go, we say hello. Ola, bonjour, hello - Hello has different names - in English, French or Spanish. Let's greet our friends from all around the world with a nice hello in their language.

programme.title: Draco

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Tony Blackburn presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 9 June 1977 and featuring Osibisa, Berni Flint, The Wurzels, Neil Innes, Demis Roussos, Bob Marley and the Wailers, ELO, Frankie Miller, The Stranglers, Honky and Rod Stewart, with a dance sequence from Legs & Co.

programme.title: Omnibus

programme.title: Tina Turner at the BBC

programme.title: When Tina Turner Came to Britain
programme.desc: Using interviews and told in her own words, this is the story of Tina Turner's 50-year relationship with the UK, also featuring interviews with British musicians and fans who shared her journey. From her first visit, alongside Ike Turner, supporting the Rolling Stones in 1966, to her legendary 1983 comeback performance on C4's `The Tube' and working with Heaven 17, Tina has always had a soft spot for the UK.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 17 December 1999 and featuring Tom Jones & Cerys Matthews, B*Witched & Ladysmith Black Mambazo, Progress Presents The Boy Wunda, Vengaboys, Artful Dodger feat Craig David and Cliff Richard.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Tony Blackburn presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 9 June 1977 and featuring Osibisa, Berni Flint, The Wurzels, Neil Innes, Demis Roussos, Bob Marley and the Wailers, ELO, Frankie Miller, The Stranglers, Honky and Rod Stewart, with a dance sequence from Legs & Co.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Richard Skinner present the pop chart programme, first shown on 6 June 1985. Featuring Gary Moore & Phil Lynott, Kool and the Gang, Propaganda, David Bowie, Duran Duran and Paul Hardcastle.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Simon Mayo present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 9 June 1988 and featuring the Timelords, Five Star, Voice of the Beehive, Mica Paris, Fields of the Nephilim, Sabrina, James Brown, Maxi Priest, Morrissey, Wet Wet Wet and Erasure.

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: Film profile of singer and pianist Ray Charles. Featuring Charles in conversation with Charlie Gillett and clips from his 1986 Royal Festival Hall concert and earlier performances.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: When Chelsea goes to make a coffee, she notices her milk hasn't been delivered. She reviews her doorbell footage and is shocked to discover a man breaking into her house all while she was asleep upstairs. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan shows how even fancy flats are targets for thieves. And Anabela is gobsmacked when she discovers her cat has been stolen, but will turning to social media manage to recover him?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Have an animal-loving couple done enough research at a plot of land in Leicestershire? Quirky properties in North Yorkshire and Norfolk also go under the hammer with intriguing results!

programme.title: MOTD FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.sub-title: Group B: Canada v Bosnia and Herzegovina
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening match in Group B between host nation Canada and Bosnia and Herzegovina at Toronto Stadium. Canada are playing at their third World Cup, having previously appeared in 1986 and 2022, but have yet to win a game. Bosnia and Herzegovina qualified through the European play-offs, beating Wales and Italy to clinch their place. This is the first ever meeting between the two teams.

programme.desc: Matt's first birthday party gets crashed by a rival, and Joyce decides to "get back out there."

programme.title: Wedding Crashers
programme.desc: John Beckwith and Jeremy Grey are a pair of committed philanderers who sneak into weddings in a bid to pick up women.

programme.title: MOTD FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening match in Group D between host nation USA and Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium. USA, now led by former Tottenham and Chelsea manager Mauricio Pochettino, reached the last 16 as hosts in 1994. Paraguay have reached the World Cup for the first time since 2010, when they reached a maiden quarter-final before being eliminated by eventual champions Spain.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents this high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team featuring Olympian and TV presenter Greg Rutherford, comedian and actor Sanjeev Kohli, presenter and writer Janet Ellis and actor Louisa Lytton as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: The lands of the north bloom as nearly 24 hours of daylight transform the snow-clad landscapes. Arctic fox clans struggle to raise their cubs on Iceland's volcanic shores. On Shetland it's time for the otter cub to strike out alone, while a lone puffin also searches out a partner. And in the mountains tiny reindeer calves take their first steps. As the season moves on, orca pods arrive in Shetland to hunt harbour seals, and fledgling seabirds take a leap of faith.

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major visit Alloa, a town famous for brewing and glassmaking. Ian takes on a judo champion and tries the beer at the town's last remaining brewery.

programme.title: Wha's Like Us
programme.desc: Sanjeev Kohli takes a humorous look at how films have shown Scots, the Scots character and stereotypes, celebrating some of our most memorable onscreen moments.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: It's the middle of a harsh winter and the old boys are frozen beyond belief. In the search to find some heat are they desperate enough to break the law?

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: With their house in turmoil in the midst of the redecoration, new Latimer Crescent residents Alan and Michelle take refuge from the floor sanding at Beth and Eric's house. Michelle is keen that they are not going to be a nuisance and tries to get to bed as early as possible, but just as she heads upstairs, Christine arrives bearing rhubarb. Hot on her heels are Cathy and Colin, who turn up with the leftovers from their restaurant meal.

programme.title: Janey Godley: Still Got It
programme.desc: Filmed in 2022 at Glasgow's Websters Theatre during her chemotherapy treatment, Janey Godley faces up to her mistakes in a hilarious, touching and scorchingly honest stand-up comedy performance: a landmark moment in her life and career.

programme.title: Short Stuff
programme.desc: More new comedy from Scotland, featuring the brightest comic talent.

programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Timor Leste in search of giants.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Mervin and the team investigate the suspicious death of a wellness business owner after she suffers an allergic reaction at the launch of her skincare product.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A daring night mission causes group tension and isolates Pip. Jamie's case stalls until Pip decodes a clue that cracks it wide open. In court, the verdict is given.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: As they chase the truth, Pip and her friends face new risks and shocking discoveries. Will they find Jamie and make it through unharmed?

programme.title: Planet Sex With Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Porn is everywhere. But Cara discovers it isn't all bad. In Spain, she helps make an ethical, feminist porn film. She explores positive role models in gay porn in Germany, boy-on-boy love comics for women in Japan and sensitive, homemade porn in New York.

programme.title: Planet Sex With Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Cara explores drag with a trans man in LA, discovers a third gender culture in Mexico and experiences a `gender swap' in Spain. Along the way, she confronts her own gender fluidity and masculinity.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Things are looking up both personally and professionally for Tommy, but Roman has some bad news. John decides to cheer the boys up by taking them on a camping trip.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy heads to Budapest to close the deal for the fight, but there's a hitch. With just four weeks to go, John calls the deal off because the money hasn't reached Tommy's account.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy faces his comeback fight in Budapest, and the pressure is on. How will he cope after nearly two years out of the ring?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The cars put to the test include the Aston Martin Rapide, the Porsche Panamera Turbo and the Maserati Quattroporte, in a search for the world's greatest four seat supercar. Richard is also on the test track the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG. There's a double dose of celebrity as `Harry Potter' star Rupert Grint and F1 ace Rubens Barrichello take their turns as the stars in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The BBC's biggest comedy talent search heads to the stunning Queen's University Belfast and Mandela Hall. This state-of-the-art live music, comedy and concert venue is situated in the heart of the University Quarter. Five acts will take to the stage in front of a packed house of more than 200 comedy-goers. Former NCA finalist Dane Buckley is our host.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: In the mysterious world of the Nethersphere, plans have been drawn. Missy is about to come face-to-face with the Doctor, and an impossible choice is looming.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: With Cybermen on the streets of London, old friends unite against old enemies, and the Doctor takes to the air in a startling new role. Can the mighty UNIT contain Missy?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny is threatening to cancel Miranda's Christmas if she does not sort her life out. Amongst other things, she forces her daughter on a detox. Meanwhile, Stevie has a new executive job and Miranda tries to follow her in to an office job but it all proves too much. Meanwhile, are Gary and Miranda able to just be friends?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Penny embarrasses Miranda by running for local councillor with Tilly as her wingman. Gary has put himself on the dating scene and Miranda decides she has to do the same - they both want dates for Stevie's birthday party. Will they find partners or end up back in each other's company? The result is a big surprise for Stevie.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Suspicion surrounds Cathy's creepy boyfriend, Boris. Buster and Dermot are convinced he is a vampire and hatch a plan to save Cathy, which leaves her mortified. Sharon has boyfriend troubles of her own, and Father Damien has been called away to tend to some depressed chickens. Meanwhile, Buster convinces Agnes she should have her antique vase valued.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Mrs Brown and the gang start the New Year by attempting a tough new health and fitness regime. New year, new Agnes!

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: The three funboys, Callum Brown, Jordan McCafferty and Lorcan Boggin, may have taken the scenic route through emotional development but they're trying to make the most of life in rural Ballymacnoose. While working at the local shop, Callum acquires his first-ever female admirer in the form of Gemma, who is new to town and seeking friends. Unfortunately, Callum's burgeoning relationship means that he no longer wants to play video games all weekend with the fellas.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Ballymacnoose
programme.desc: After a gas leak at his flat, Callum moves in with Lorcan and his dad, Sammy, aka the biggest git in Ballymacnoose. Driven by a desire to no longer be a pushover and in a desperate attempt to get over his break-up with Gemma, Callum goes under Sammy's wing and unleashes his own inner git. Meanwhile, Jordan's two dads are finally tying the knot and Jordan is caught up in all the excitement of the wedding.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: With Callum now unwelcome at Boggins' farm and his flat still out of bounds because of a gas leak, he turns once more to his oldest and dearest friend, Jordan. Things seem to be going well and Jordan's two dads show Callum some much-needed sympathy.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Gemma and Lorcan throw a party at Boggins' farm and it is the biggest social event in Ballymacnoose with a whole eight people attending. Callum makes a new friend but there is a catch.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna needs a man so her mum won't think she's repellent to the opposite sex. Janet wants to help, but discovers that matchmaking can seriously damage your boyfriend.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny tries to be horrible to Janet and fails miserably. Donna is after as much sex with as little commitment as possible. Flo, however, has other plans, and Louise discovers that dieting can damage the buttocks.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz finds Donna's toothbrush in his flat and realises he has to stop her merging, and Janet's plan to make Jonny fancy her backfires.

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Exploring the negative impact of Instagram on young women's body image.

programme.desc: Zoe leaves Dan to babysit while the friends go to a 'Gods and Monsters' party thrown by local ex-pats. But nothing is what it seems, and Zoe is in danger of straying too far.

programme.desc: A vulnerable elderly man sparks an island-wide search for answers, a roadside stop uncovers a lethal switchblade, and airport officers track suspicious luggage.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: Caring for the curious collections at two of the National's Trust's most unusual houses, from a boy's suit of samurai armour to stuffed birds and a living collection of rare sheep.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is on the last stretch of his railway adventure through the Balkan countries of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Croatia. programme.desc: Slim and Queen's first date takes an unexpected turn when a policeman pulls them over for a minor traffic violation. When the situation escalates, Slim takes the officer's gun and shoots him in self-defence. Now labelled cop killers in the media, Slim and Queen feel that they have no choice but to go on the run and evade the law. When a video of the incident goes viral, the unwitting outlaws soon become a symbol of trauma, terror, grief and pain for people all across the country.

programme.desc: Duck must make sure everyone follows the rules at a campsite - even Frog!

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: JJ applies for an elite sporting school. However, not realising it's miles away from Beanotown, he needs to do badly in the sporting assessment to get out of it.

programme.title: Bitesize: 5-7 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about tempo, dynamics and how to use them both.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Cam, Tyler and Aaron want to hold a superhero-themed party inspired by the secret superpowers of some of the zoo's most incredible animals. Rica the armadillo comes out of her shell to show off her super-sensitive sniff, and Moby the eagle owl performs awesome aerobatics without making a sound. Meanwhile, Bactrian camels Mo and Navas display some serious suction with their super-slurping, and Cletus the dwarf crocodile arrives at the zoo ready to flex his pecs.

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: The Kwapongs get noisy with homemade instruments, Aowen Jin paints UV art that glows, and Emily Steel - aka Gladiator Dynamite - flexes her muscles drawing a weightlifting alpaca!

programme.title: Art Ninja

While Sarah and Rich make a splash with their singing and dancing, Ricky makes a drum kit desk tidy, a bowl inspired by Matisse and then uses his skills to make rubbish art that's anything but trash.

programme.title: Absolute Genius With Dick and Dom
programme.desc: Dick and Dom reveal the genius of Sir Joseph Bazalgette, the man who invented London's giant sewer system. Inspired by Bazalgette's genius idea, Dick and Dom come up with their own genius idea to demonstrate the explosive power of poo.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A lazy aristocrat invents the sandwich, the Romans host their own revolting cookery show and William Shakespeare is haunted by the ghost of King Richard III.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.desc: It's a windy day and Bing wants to fly his Hoppity Voosh kite with Flop. And he wants to do it all by himself. When the kite gets caught up in the park bench, Bing realises that there are some times when another pair of hands comes in handy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies wait for tubby toast, then they wait for the Tubby Phone to be answered and finally they wait for the Tiddlytubbies to go to sleepybyes. A boy in Tummy Tales waits to cross a road with his grandad and waits for a bus.

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee's a growing monkey and is getting quite heavy, however some days he just wants to be carried, all day.

programme.title: Supertato

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Nicky are sleeping over at Sula's house, but Bing has forgotten Hoppity. The wind causes a branch to make a scratchy noise that terrifies Bing, who isn't very comforted by Sula's fairy rhino. Amma phones Flop, while Sula, Nicky and Bing play 'Nicky tickles' as they wait. Flop arrives, bringing his sleeping bag and Hoppity so they can all sleep over.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo have to make sure Dot doesn't see her present before her birthday party. But that's easier said than done when Dot becomes an unstoppable present-hunting machine.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Help the Squirrels and Detective Puppy solve the case about the mysterious milkshake.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels have made a song. They enjoy playing for others but wish they had a chance to see how they perform together. Duggee explains that they can if they make a music video, which tells a story. There is a lot to prepare beforehand, such as lights, costumes and glitter, and as they prepare, we are immersed into Enid's dream, where she gives a star performance in her own eclectic music video!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey wants to know why Dad's always bossing her around when she doesn't get what she wants.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Mum and Dad have a disagreement, Bluey makes Dad write a love letter to Mum to make up. Now Bluey must find a way to deliver the letter to Mum.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Kyan wants to climb Mount Everest, but there's a giant snowball heading his way - and it's got a moustache!

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: The Octonauts venture into a dangerous red-hot lava tube to rescue a teeny tiny water bear who doesn't seem to mind the heat at all!

programme.title: Piripenguins

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift make a brilliant new friend with a magnificent roar, Steggy the Dinosaur.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula have a penny each for the surprise machine in Padget's shop. Sula gets the silver rhino she wanted, so Bing is convinced he will get the alien he wants.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'milk' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. There's white milk, pink milk, baby milk and banana milk. We snuggle up for bedtime milk and even enjoy some mighty milk and monster milk! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way and everyone has a boogie when Dee's friend finally succeeds in saying today's word!

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu upsets Elodie when she laughs at an involuntary noise she makes.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri has to overcome her jealousy when everyone gives Lou presents.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos see a lady putting coloured stars next to items in a shop window display, and getting carried away putting stars next to everything in sight! The Stop-Go Car brings some blocks, and the Twirlywoos have fun lining them up.

programme.title: Something Special: We're All Friends
programme.desc: Justin spends the day at his friend's school. They have lots of fun in the classroom, meeting the school dog and eating pizza. Meanwhile, it is Tumble Ted's first day at school and Mr Tumble pretends to be the teacher.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Chip sings a heartfelt song about being a potato. Though he can't do many physical things, he is proud of who he is.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dispy, Laa-Laa and Po enjoy Tiddlytubbies Songtime. Then it is time for Teletubbies Songtime. The Teletubbies sing solo before singing all together.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Joseph, Baani, Jean, Amber, Amelia, Luke and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside everyone's bags are dollies which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding a safe, baby appropriate dolly inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby!

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children make twiggy stars and sign and sing 'Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star'.

programme.title: What's in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Food stylist Rob introduces the Fact Pack to edible flowers. They discover that some special flowers can be eaten, how different flowers taste, and how edible flowers can make food look and taste exciting.

programme.title: Hamza Loves Animals: Africa
programme.desc: Baby rhino Maisha is in a bad mood, but her friends help her feel better. Hamza spots some fantastic colobus monkeys in the trees and searches for clues to find the elephants.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: When Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes' yummy drops get snatched by a group of Beetrootsaurus, so begins a game of pea-in-the-middle to get them back.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Indy and Rusty play a game of Mums and Dads, they can't agree on who goes to work.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are admiring everything Duggee has grown in his vegetable garden, especially his crop of giant carrots, when they hear a noise.

programme.title: Colourblocks
programme.desc: Red and Blue meet Yellow and they have fun colouring the seaside.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Jasiri finds out some fascinating frog facts. Rory and his son Sandy hop like frogs to the local lochside and make a pond in their garden. Ferne visits an aquarium to meet some amazing amphibians, including poison dart frogs.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Amanpreet challenges her sister Amrit to a biking competition to see who can cycle around the bike track the fastest. The bikes get really muddy, so Amanpreet takes charge of giving them a good clean and has some splashy fun.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Diellza's family comes from Kosovo. She makes a traditional recipe of stuffed peppers. Diellza has lovely colourful ingredients for her stuffed peppers such as spring onions, sweetcorn, grated carrot.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie explores how our body works. She visits a children's hospital to find out what happens when we have an X-ray. She meets James, who has broken his arm, as well as a radiographer. We see what happens when we have an X-ray and learn how the X-ray machine takes a picture of the bones inside our body before saving the picture on a special machine. Next, Maddie heads to a factory where they make socks in lots of different colours out of yarn.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Guide dog Kika uses her super sight to help Dr Amit and his friend Alana play a game of sound tennis. Kika tries not to chase the noisy balls at the sports centre.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie:Giant Chinese Salamander and Alaotran Gentle Lemur
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet Chinese giant salamanders and Alaotran gentle lemurs.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle's forgotten to do homework and Mr Nolan decides if he can continue as a pupil. Jess gets a text from Evie's birth dad.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona, Baba and Mossy babysit a little egg and help find its mama and papa.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.sub-title: I Just Want to Be Me
programme.desc: The gang sing a punk rock anthem about how wonderful it is to just be yourself.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes has written a brilliant song, but his guitar string is broken, so he can't play it. Yukee decides to surprise Stripes by enlisting his garden friends to sing the song to him. The trouble is they can't find the right pitch to sing the song in!

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie, an adventurous youngster who happens to be visually impaired, sets out with her best doggy friend Triggs to discover the music and adventure in the sounds of the world around them.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels notice that Happy looks and seems different - he is frowning, his smile is upside down, and he's wearing new trainers! When they check in on him, Happy is frustrated, but he can't seem to identify where his frustration is coming from. They turn to Duggee for help, who uses his feeling cards to explain an array of feelings to Happy. All the Squirrels help by relaying their past experiences and the feelings that they evoked.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: One finds a friendly furball, two of them tickle Two and soon Numberland is full of fun and fluffies. Have fun counting and learning how to make sevens with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: S is feeling a bit sad but T, A and P are here to help and before long, everyone is tap dancing. Oh dear! T has no tea, A has too many apples but S is sagging so the others want to help. P pops in and they try some word magic - perhaps a comforting pat or a big musical number? This episode introduces the letters S, A, T and P and the words 'sat', 'pat', 'tap' and 'taps'.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend have fun at the ski slope.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dispy, Laa-Laa and Po have their favourite things. The bag, the hat, the scooter and the ball all magically swap places and become mixed up.

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels back to the time of Iberomesornis - a bird that lived in the forests of Spain 127 million years ago. His mission, to find some blue feathers belonging to the bird to replace the one he and Hatty accidentally lost back at the museum. Along the way he takes to the skies with one of the largest flying pterosaurs that ever lived. But, will he succeed in time before the museum opens?

programme.title: Ansoo Ban Gaye Phool
programme.desc: When honest Vidyanand is sent to prison by a Seth who bears a grudge against him, he has only one thing on his mind - to avenge his humiliation, and he will go to any extent to achieve this.

programme.title: Swades
programme.desc: Mohan Bhargava is a successful NRI (non-resident Indian) working at the technological forefront of NASA in the USA. Driven by nostalgia, he returns to his roots to find the nanny who raised him in his homeland, intending to bring her back to America. His journey leads him into the rural heart of India, to the village of Charanpur, and sets him on both an outward and inner quest to find where he truly belongs.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Candy

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo
programme.desc: Ross unexpectedly finds himself looking after Ben and decides to do something special for Christmas. Since all the Santa costumes have been rented out, Ross dons the best outfit left and entertains Ben as the holiday armadillo.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Cheesecakes
programme.desc: Chandler and Rachel pig out on a neighbour's cheesecake that was mistakenly delivered to Chandler. They feel bad, but not quite guilty enough to refrain from eating a second one that has also been wrongly delivered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They're Up All Night
programme.desc: During their fist night together, Rachel and Tag realise they forgot to ship out an important package at the office.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's new job as a telemarketer gets off to a tricky start when the first person she calls, an office worker called Earl, tells her he feels unappreciated and wants to kill himself. programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They All Turn Thirty
programme.desc: Celebrating Rachel's 30th birthday inspires the friends to reminisce about their own 30th birthdays. programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joey's New Brain
programme.desc: Joey accidentally informs a cast member that she will be fired so that his character will get her character's brain, and Ross plans a surprise for Monica and Chandler's wedding - he wants to play the bagpipes.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Truth About London
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler fondly reminisce about London until a startling revelation leaves Chandler floored. Meanwhile, Rachel teaches Ben practical jokes to play on Ross.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Cheap Wedding Dress

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: Winter is approaching and Billy faces a new medical crisis. Plus, a big storm forces Noah and Rhain Alisha to make a tough decision.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns struggle to finish their homes as the ground begins to freeze. Later, Rhain-Alisha goes into labour and the Wolfpack race to be there for the birth.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns kick off an ambitious plan. Bear and the siblings throw caution to the wind on a critical first project disassembling and transporting a 50-foot windmill.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: A hungry bear breaks into Noah's tent and terrifies the livestock. Later, the windmill assembly takes the siblings to dangerous new heights.

programme.title: Sara Price: Chasing Dakar

programme.title: Deadliest Catch

programme.title: In the Eye of the Storm: Chasers
programme.desc: Six teams of chasers brave Oklahoma back roads as a late spring warm front unleashes long-track tornadoes.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Dark matter and energy battle for the cosmos and the winner will determine the universe's fate. New discoveries may reveal which force will win.

programme.title: How the Universe Works

Gordon quickly realises that it's going to need something approaching a miracle if he's going to have a chance of saving the place.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa signs up for a one-way ticket to Mars, forcing Marge to employ a devious strategy to keep her daughter on Earth.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer makes it his mission to find Smithers a boyfriend and signs him up to a dating app. Meanwhile, Bart messes with the school's stage show.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride Ireland

With some advice from a naked stranger, a dodgy wedding veil, a sports club reception, can this football crazy groom really make this bride's dreams come true?

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride Ireland
programme.desc: Breda and Paddy's love story began two years ago and over 8000 miles away in Australia. Now the couple live in Cashel with their baby daughter Emily. Paddy predicted that organising the perfect wedding would be a walk in the park, but with a ceremony in a bog, a reception venue filled with snakes and kangaroos, and the bride is arriving to her wedding on a turf cutter, will Paddy really pull off Breda's dream wedding, or will this day get dragged through the mud?

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: President and CEO of Taco Bueno, Mike Roper, goes undercover in his own company to work out how he can double the number of restaurants in the company.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The CEO of Associa, John Carona, goes undercover in his own company. John recognises the importance of growth and retention in a competitive industry that is rapidly changing, so he has introduced new technology to the business.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: On a trip to Greenville in Mississippi, Gordon has just 24 hours to give Sherman's, a family-owned fine dining establishment, a bright farmhouse chic interior and a modernised menu.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon visits Los Angeles, California, to save La Serenata, a family-owned Mexican restaurant that has been an LA staple for over three decades. Gordon and his team have just 24 hours to update the dining room and plan smaller more dynamic menu.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: There's No Business Like Snow Business
programme.desc: The treasure seekers travel to the mountain community of Rimforest, California, where hot bidding meets cold temperatures. Ivy goes on a buying spree, and Casey attempts to keep Rene's eyes on the prize.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: A stop in West Covina, California, finds Mary desperate for a big score. Ivy attempts to lighten the mood, Jarrod and Brandi get back to basics, and Rene unleashes his bankroll.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle, there's wine snobbery, a fairy-tale feast and a Moroccan menu based solely on one couple's love of hummus.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The five stout-hearted souls who have willingly put their reputations on the line in search of glory are Fatiha El-Ghorri, Jason Mantzoukas, Mathew Baynton, Rosie Ramsey and Stevie Martin.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr is joined by Sean Lock and David O'Doherty who go up against Jon Richardson and Natasia Demetriou. John Kearns joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Dad annoys everyone with his old ventriloquist dummy Lord Luck, while Jonny's recently dumped friend Ben comes for dinner in need of cheering up.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam is not happy when he is forced to play his violin for Mum and her best friend Val, while Jonny is relishing every moment. Meanwhile, Dad is obsessed with his old microscope.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark gets into online gaming and live role play with Dobby and wonders whether she may be `the one'. Later, Mark and Sophie finally sort out their annulment, while Jeremy is brainwashed, loosely speaking, and joins a cult.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: The award-winning sitcom returns, with Mark and Jeremy desperate to enjoy their final days of freedom before Sophie's baby arrives. Mark, newly promoted at work, decides to enjoy his additional power and wealth by splashing out on a boiler, a `creamy elephant' sofa, and by finding Jeremy a job in the office. Feeling on top of his game, he also decides to pursue Dobby, but a fire drill soon dampens his passions.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers on Holiday
programme.desc: A noughties heartthrob visits the parlour. Glen transforms Dave's shoddy shot glass. Sketch gives Zoe a bespoke design. Alice gets rid of Jodie's bad decision.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The five stout-hearted souls who have willingly put their reputations on the line in search of glory are Fatiha El-Ghorri, Jason Mantzoukas, Mathew Baynton, Rosie Ramsey and Stevie Martin.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr is joined by Sean Lock and David O'Doherty who go up against Jon Richardson and Natasia Demetriou. John Kearns joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the north east of England, Kevin and Lalita's hearty portions prove a challenge. Michelle and Charlotte host with an Asian theme. And Richard and Christine serve dinner party classics.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lara and Greg serve a travel-inspired menu. Andrew and Liz attempt a dress rehearsal for their wedding breakfast. And Jenna and Mitch prove that looks can be deceptive.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Hardworking Hayley is plunged out of her depth when she hands over control of her big day to a man with a crazy underwater plan. Her fiance Ian has just three weeks and £12,000 to pull off the wedding of a lifetime. Working as a sports physio, Hayley dreams of hanging up her tracksuit and becoming a gypsy princess, but hopeless romantic Ian wants to throw her in at the deep end of their local leisure centre.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Liverpool lad Terry plans a wedding in three weeks for Northern Irish beauty, Melissa. While controlling bride Melissa dreams of a traditional Irish wedding, happy-go-lucky Terry's a Ghanaian Scouser with grand plans to celebrate his African roots. After disastrous efforts to plan their wedding together, Melissa fears that unless she hands control over to Terry they will split up for good.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Another brave bride leaves the most important day of her life in the hands of the man she loves. Fun-loving prison officer Noel has three weeks to plan a wedding for his long-suffering bride-to-be, Jemma. Happy-go-lucky Noel and prison teacher Jemma first met two years ago and less than a year into their relationship Jemma fell pregnant unexpectedly.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Professional wrestler Mike wants to plan a knockout wedding for his bride-to-be Becky, while she yearns for a girlyfest full of style and elegance. They haven't been together long, but Mike wants to prove to Becky how much he loves her and in return Becky is trusting him with £12,000 and her dream wedding.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Some of the brides recall their nuptials and get their own back on the grooms for dropping wedding clangers with a yuletide set of embarrassing forfeits.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: True Value CEO John Hartmann, a former FBI agent, goes under cover and is shocked by the antics of an employee.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Pittsburgh Mayor Bill Peduto tackles undercover roles of municipal employees including a refuse worker, a carpenter and a self-sufficiency coordinator.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon visits Los Angeles, California, to save La Serenata, a family-owned Mexican restaurant that has been an LA staple for over three decades. Gordon and his team have just 24 hours to update the dining room and plan smaller more dynamic menu.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: In this episode, Gordon Ramsay travels to Wichita, Kansas in an attempt to save Fetch Bistro, the first dog-friendly restaurant in the state.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: At Hermosa Beach in California, Gordon attempts to save Patrick Molloy's, a family-owned Irish pub, by refurbishing the dining room and creating a simplified and delicious menu.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: At Sacramento in California, Gordon helps out BBQ and seafood restaurant Sandra Dee's. He and his team have just 24 hours to renovate the premises by updating the 20-year-old dining room's faded decor and overhauling the kitchen and menu.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits classic Cajun-inspired restaurant the Trolley Stop Café, located in the heart of New Orleans, Louisiana, to clean and remodel the interior and revamp the menu.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host a compilation of best bits from the anarchic letters and numbers game, featuring Sean Lock, Bob Mortimer, Harry Hill, Joe Lycett, Jon Richardson, Lucy Beaumont, Katherine Ryan and more.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Alan Carr and Harriet Kemsley play against Jonathan Ross and Maisie Adam. Huge Davies joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum head to Portland, Oregon where cannabis is legal to use recreationally. As their trip quite literally goes to pot, will Ninette have a go on the demon weed?

programme.title: Russell Howard & Mum: USA Road Trip
programme.desc: Russell and his mum arrive in Las Vegas to help judge the legendary Mrs Globe Beauty Pageant. However, Russell must first understand what it takes to be a beauty queen.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a brand-new series of the topical panel show with new team captains Rob Beckett and Aisling Bea. In this show, Rob and Aisling are joined in their quest to uncover the nation's most popular talking points by First Date's love guru Fred Siriex and This Is England actor Thomas Turgoose.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr is joined by new team captains Rob Beckett and Aisling Bea and guests Melanie Melanie C, Drifters' star Jesicca Knappett and comedians Joe Wilkinson and Tom Allen to discuss the topics that set the great British public's chins wagging.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host a compilation of best bits from the anarchic letters and numbers game, featuring Sean Lock, Bob Mortimer, Harry Hill, Joe Lycett, Jon Richardson, Lucy Beaumont, Katherine Ryan and more.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Erica wins a bet with Beverly, she decides to opt out of Thanksgiving, leaving her mom trying to find away to get her back. Uncle Marvin visits and unexpectedly bonds with Adam.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam's exaggeration about how he broke his arm could jeopardise his friendship with Emmy Mirsky. Beverly fears she is being replaced when the family falls in love with a new restaurant.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Craig's grand plan for his wedding to Sofia was to combine his two great loves: her and beer. He threw an Oktoberfest-themed do, complete with short leather pants and steins full of lager. The pair relive the moments that led up to their big day.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell isn't sure how to tell Cam that his mother makes him feel uncomfortable, Haley finally breaks up with Dylan and Jay has a little stomach ache.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Jay continues to adjust to life with his young Latino bride and her sensitive son, Claire tries to keep a leash on husband Phil and their brood, and gay couple Mitch and Cam experience the marvels and mayhem of bringing up their adopted Vietnamese baby.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Spencer and Sara host a simple Jewish feast. Lauren and Jack's menu reflects their South African and Burmese roots. Paul and Ed's flavoursome food proves problematic.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: The first part of a two-part visit to the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont, a place that looks like a million dollars but smells of raw sewage. Gordon meets the inn's wealthy owner, Robert Dean II, and uncovers some shocking treatment towards the staff. Gordon quickly realises that it's going to need something approaching a miracle if he's going to have a chance of saving the place.

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: This programme sees Mr Ramsay's greatest challenge to date, as he continues to uncover problem after problem at the Juniper Hill Inn in Windsor, Vermont. He tries to help the staff communicate with owners Robert and Ari, but after catching one of them in a shocking lie, Gordon feels he has no choice but to abandon ship.

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean
programme.desc: "Below Deck Mediterranean" voyages into uncharted waters this season as Captain Sandy Yawn navigates a new boat, new crew members, and a new dazzling aquatic locale.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil gets an attractive new client and tries to keep her a secret from Claire. Meanwhile, Claire gets attention from her hot new yoga teacher.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil decides he wants to take a spontaneous trip to Paris with Claire, but they are both exhausted, Jay's command of the bathroom remodel creates hazards for the rest of the family.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa writes a new anthem for Springfield when she learns the original was stolen from another town, and Homer learns the meaning of `wide pride'.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is asked to keep Princess Kemi out of trouble while her father, a Nigerian king, makes a deal with Mr. Burns; Moe thinks Kemi is related to the Nigerian prince who scammed him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Chief Wiggum uses a military jet pack to fight crime, he ends up crashing it into the church, leaving Marge and the congregation to resort to gambling to raise money for the repairs.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge follows Bart around in an effort to get him to confess to being involved in a bulldozer crash; Homer ignores Santa's Little Helper when Flanders brings a new dog home.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The whole squad is given a job to do for the wedding ceremony for Gina and Charles' parents, but a criminal distracts Amy and Jake.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Jake's father (Bradley Whitford) comes to town, Jake is excited to see him, but Charles is wary of his intentions; Holt challenges Amy, Terry, Gina and Rosa with a brain teaser in exchange for Beyonce tickets.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: After seeing an online video of his Fizbo costume being desecrated, Cam sets out to restore the character's good name; Phil meets his soon-to-be stepbrother; Manny enlists Gloria, Claire and Haley to do a read-through of his newest play.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: NBA stars Charles Barkley and DeAndre Jordan attend the charity basketball game Phil is playing in; Gloria's nemesis tries to shame her for lack of support at Manny's school; Claire tries to keep a work mistake secret from Jay.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell accidentally knocks over an urn containing the ashes of Cam's beloved pet pig, Lilly; Cam tries to catch Mitchell in a lie.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitch and Cam fight the effects of sleeping pills while rushing to catch a flight; Haley and Rainer re-evaluate their relationship; Phil and Claire meet Alex's new boyfriend when they pay her a surprise visit.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart offers a talented homeless woman the chance to live in his wardrobe. Homer messes up when he tries to prove his DIY skills.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa discovers her calling after saving a raccoon. There are consequences for Marge when she moonlights as a crime scene cleaner.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa signs up for a one-way ticket to Mars, forcing Marge to employ a devious strategy to keep her daughter on Earth.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer makes it his mission to find Smithers a boyfriend and signs him up to a dating app. Meanwhile, Bart messes with the school's stage show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is sent to the Springfield Correctional Facility where he becomes friends with a tough inmate.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tries to cheer Homer up by winning him a `MyPad' at the school auction. He soon falls back into a funk when he breaks the magical tablet device, but Ned Flanders discovers a miracle tree in the Simpsons backyard.

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean
programme.desc: A massive changeover rocks the boat; a flirty love triangle starts to heat up; the chef struggles to cater to vegan guests with particular palates and complaints.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible -- Ghost Protocol
programme.desc: After the IMF are implicated in the bombing of the Kremlin, Ethan Hunt and his new team go rogue. In order to clear their organisation's name, they track down a dangerous terrorist named Kurt Hendricks. Hendricks has gained access to Russian nuclear launch codes and is planning a strike on the United States, but will the new team be able to stop the impending disaster?

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Professional dancer and virgin Raheem wants a man who can pop some moves on the dance floor and in the bedroom. Rugby player Jerome needs a confidence boost to find his lucky lady.

programme.title: Dating Naked UK
programme.desc: After the shock of the dumping ground, two daters struggle to adjust to the change in the house. A fancy dress party delivers a special naked treat and too much tongue action pushes one pair to breaking point.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: How will Adele react when Matt tells her that his passion for Liverpool FC led to his divorce? Meanwhile, Max and Esther bond over a shared love of words and is Ben the nice guy Leora's looking for?

programme.title: Magnificent Obsession
programme.desc: Dr Bob Merrick is responsible for the death of a loved and dedicated doctor. The doctor's wife spurns his remorse but then she is blinded in an accident. Merrick, deciding to do the decent thing, trains as a physician and eventually performs the sight-restoring surgery on her. She falls in love with him while blind, never realising his true identity but, on seeing him, will she realise how he has changed?

programme.title: The Major and the Minor
programme.desc: A working girl's scheme to pose as a 12-year-old train passenger leads to romance with a gallant Army officer.

programme.title: D-Day, the Sixth of June
programme.desc: It is the night before D-Day. One ship, carrying Special Force Six, leaves ahead of the main invasion on a dangerous mission. On board are British Colonel Wynter and American Captain Parker, who each, in flashback, reminisce about their separate involvement with beauteous Valerie Russell.

programme.title: The Longest Day
programme.desc: The heroics and tragedies of the Allied invasion of Normandy, from the tense wait for the weather to break before the huge armada could set sail, to the bloody battles fought on the beaches of France.

programme.title: Commando
programme.desc: The ex-leader of an elite military task force is pressed back into service when his daughter is kidnapped.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: In the shadow of a dark internet conspiracy theory, a group of elitists gathers at a remote location to hunt humans for sport. But their master plan is about to be derailed when one of the hunted, Crystal, turns the tables on her pursuers.

programme.title: Hush
programme.desc: On a night-time motorway journey, young couple Zakes and Beth catch a horrifying glimpse of a young woman imprisoned in the back of a truck. When the two stop at a service station, Beth goes missing and a terrified Zakes races to track her down, fearing she has become the driver's next victim.

programme.title: The Warrior
programme.desc: A warlord's warrior in ancient India who is sent to massacre a village behind in their taxes suffers a crisis of conscience and, laying down his sword, sets off to the Himalayas in search of peace. The vengeful warlord sends enforcers on the trail of his renegade warrior and he must once again face violence.

programme.title: The Frogmen
programme.desc: Powerful and engrossing World War II tale centred on the exploits of frogmen in an underwater demolition team, headed by Lieutenant Commander John Lawrence, as they fight the Japanese.

programme.title: Bridge of Spies: Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Steven Spielberg and stars Tom Hanks and Mark Rylance talk about making their Cold War espionage drama.

programme.title: Spies in Disguise
programme.desc: A charming super spy and his geeky scientist companion are almost exact opposites. But what the scientist lacks in social skills he makes up for in invention, creating awesome gadgets for the spy to use on his epic missions.

programme.title: The Lavender Hill Mob
programme.desc: Henry Holland is a fussy supervisor who oversees gold bullion deliveries to the bank in which he works. Secretly, he is plotting to steal a load of bullion and retire early, but he cannot figure out a way to smuggle it out of the country.

programme.title: Sister Act
programme.desc: A cabaret singer witnesses a murder and goes undercover as a nun. In the convent, she is put in charge of coaching the choir, which she transforms into a toe-tapping rhythm group. As a result, the church's congregation swells until even the Pope comes to witness pop classics such as `My Guy' performed to glorify the Lord, but of course, her fame gives away her hiding place to the killer.

programme.title: Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit
programme.desc: Sequel in which nightclub singer Deloris Van Cartier is asked to get back into her habit when her old friends at St Catherine's Convent find difficulty in controlling a school filled with juvenile delinquents. Convincing everyone that Deloris is a nun is going to be a challenge in itself, especially with a group of incompetent monks trying to hinder their efforts.

programme.title: Braveheart
programme.desc: William Wallace is the medieval Scottish patriot who is spurred into revolt against the English when the love of his life is slaughtered. Leading his army into battles that become a war, his advance into England threatens King Edward I's throne before he is captured and executed, but not before becoming a symbol for a free Scotland.

programme.title: Benedetta
programme.desc: A 17th-century nun becomes entangled in a forbidden lesbian affair, but it's her shocking religious visions that threaten to shake the Church to its very core.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James is bringing his culinary tour of the UK to his neck of the woods as he spends precious time in his adopted home county of Hampshire. He's taking his gun dog Cooper to a training session at Laverstoke before cooking an amazing seasonal dish of partridge with vegetables and a sloe gin sauce. James hangs out with his best friend Jody Scheckter, an ex-formula one racer with an epic car collection, and a surprising passion for buffalo mozzarella.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Soothing and much-loved home comfort delights, from the very simple to the extremely decadent. Mary indulges her love of comfort food with homely recipes to warm the heart including eggs benedict florentine, fall-off-the-bone slow-cooked lamb shanks and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots. She invites Swedish fire-chef Niklas Esktedt to share the joys of cave-man cooking with her, and enjoys his inventive venison meatballs with blackened celeriac and mouth-watering Swedish donuts.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Mary returns to school to celebrate her light, easy and delicious cooking. From a simple version of the classic kedgeree and some delightful breakfast drop scones, to a moreish chicken, avocado and bacon salad and deliciously easy beef burgers. Mary takes her inspiration from a visit to a groundbreaking primary school in south London which has built a secret garden where they teach their students how to grow vegetables and look after a brood of chickens.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree is asking a trio of Osage's finest for feedback on some test recipes for her new deli. There are rival chicken fried steaks, two kinds of biscuits and two double-chocolate pies in the battle of the desserts.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares her best tips and tricks on everything freezer-friendly, including recipes like one dough that can be used for two types of cookies, butternut squash soup, spicy chicken spaghetti and a roasted tomatillo sauce.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Sizzling Beef Steak and Mighty Mackerel
programme.desc: On the menu is sizzling beef steak, hoi sin prawn and noodle bowls, mighty mackerel, and mixed tomato and quinoa salad.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Steak, Liver and Bacon and Mexican Tomato Soup
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up some steak, liver and bacon, bubble and squeak mash and red onion gravy, and Mexican tomato soup, chilli nachos, and veggie and feta sprinkles.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Journalist Michael Barbaro joins Ina Garten for Spicy Hermit Bars and conversation about storytelling tactics, style, and the success of his podcast, The Daily. Then, Ina shows Michael an updated Chicken Marbella that's perfect for easy entertaining.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Hoda Kotb joins Ina Garten to talk about memories and game-changing moments. Ina shows Hoda how to assemble an Easy Mezze Board with Marinated Herbed Feta that is perfect for effortless entertaining, and they make the best Fried Chicken Sandwiches.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on the final challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith, as they compete to be crowned Bake Off 2021 champion.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel makes lime yoghurt cake with rosewater and pistachios. Plus, she meets master bread maker Richard Bertinet.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' culinary adventure hits the South West of England with a visit to the food capital of this region, the vibrant city of Bristol. Here he meets up with local chef Romy Gill who gives James a guided tour of this incredible place. Their first stop is Wapping Wharf, a series of shipping containers housing everything from a butcher to a cheese-monger. After a bit of shopping, James pitches up his mobile kitchen overlooking the iconic Clifton Suspension Bridge to make a tagine of lamb chops.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: As James' British Adventure draws to a close, he is headed for Devon to hook up with his old mate Michael Caines, who just happens to be a Michelin-starred chef and owner of Lympstone Manor, a country house hotel and restaurant with stunning views over the River Exe. After a quick look around and a spot of foraging, James sets up his kitchen amongst Michael's young vineyard to make guinea fowl with heather and brambles.

programme.title: River Cottage: Road Trip
programme.desc: Hugh heads to the Lake District and into Yorkshire in search of the finest northern tea experience, and snaffles a few good cake recipes to bring back down south. His trip almost over, he's heading back to Dorset, where a busy autumn at River Cottage HQ beckons.

programme.title: Nadiya's Simple Spices
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain kicks off the series by divulging the eight simple spices she uses everyday to create a whole host of mouthwatering meals, starting with four of her favourite feel-good dishes.

programme.title: Nadiya's Simple Spices
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain serves up deliciously treaty meals to rival any takeaway. These four flavour packed dishes - a smoky aubergine pizza, a spicy spin on egg fried rice, a speedy chicken jalfrezi and kulfi ice cream biscuit bars.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Minnesota, Guy has banana cream pie and bison pot roast, a French toast leg of lamb in Virginia, and coconut curry in California.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Mexican cafe in New Jersey for chicken wings, a pizza spot in Idaho, and a Jamaican place in Nashville.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Minnesota Guy visits a meatloaf spot, in Washington, a pub that serves sauerkraut, and a beloved hot dog joint in New Jersey.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Pork sausage pizza; Salvadoran pupusa in Washington, D.C.; vegan club sandwich.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In the Lone Star State, Adam enjoys some mouth-watering, Texas-sized grub. Can he put away a 48 ounce steak?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Alaska Adam's belt-busting challenges include a seven-course meal that must be devoured in ninety minutes.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Pep Rally, Birthday Treat and Fancy Feet
programme.desc: Buddy gets into the school spirit with a cake for Sofia's school pep rally. Grace takes a special consultation for a 40th birthday cake.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Saudi Arabia And Lobster Tail Feast
programme.desc: Carlo's team takes a cake order for delivery to all the way to Saudi Arabia. Back home a fight is brewing over the bakery's signature pastry.
