programme.sub-title: From Bunny Chow to Chicken Oysters
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's digging into the unexpected! A South African spot in Black Mountain, NC, is cooking lights-out lamb curry, a chef in San Jose, CA, is bringing big flavour to beef barbacoa, and a Boynton Beach, FL, joint is serving up chicken oysters.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Kentucky Guy visits a 1951 burger drive-in, a diner in Florida for meatloaf and a 60-year-old breakfast spot in Minnesota.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a pub in Philadelphia for fried empanadas, a spot in Washington for stuffed pork chops, and a seafood joint in Maine.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree is having a night on the town with Ladd, so she's prepping dinner for the kids in advance. She makes a slam-in-the-oven mini meatballs pasta bake and a kit for make-your-own-salad, with mixed berry shortcake for dessert.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: On the menu are divine ham and leak mini quiches, garden spread finger sandwiches, lemon raspberry tarts, mini doughnut muffins and fancy hot chocolate.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's boxing night, so Ree's running her sons to town for practice. She bakes them fruity granola chocolate chip cookies and prepares a knockout dinner of Italian sloppy joes, garlic toast and a fabulous lemon meringue pie.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree works on great new ideas for cooking low carb! She prepares grilled pork tenderloin with baby bok choy, cauliflower pizza crust and spicy pizza sauce, roasted shrimp with cherry tomatoes and roasted lemon-chili broccoli.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's dinner for two with Ree and her husband, Ladd, who's turning into a cowboy cook in the kitchen! They whip up fried shrimp with cocktail sauce, spicy curly fries, grilled corn casserole and Mexican chocolate sundaes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's mash-up mania on the ranch, and Ree proves two is better than one! She merges Italian and Mexican in chicken enchilasagne, then creates courgette bread waffles, potato chip and chocolate chip cookies, and a cheeseburger salad.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue is joined in her kitchen by award-winning musician YolanDa Brown for dishes that include a classic French stew and a family favourite of YolanDa's.

programme.title: Jamie's Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver travels Britain to find out what makes our food great. He's searching for new ideas and inspiration to create his own versions of British classics. He kicks off his road-trip close to home in the East End, uncovering his family's roots in the area and tasting classic pie and mash and modern Vietnamese street food, as well as meeting a chef turning humble burgers into gourmet cuisine.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and his wingman, top chef Mark Jordan, hop aboard their motorbikes to discover the wonderful island of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake, a seafood platter fit for a king, and James uses Guernsey's wonderful dairy produce to make a delicious creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. Finally, James and Mark set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch the King of the Sea: the mighty turbot.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James discovers the delights of Dorset. He is joined on his trip by his mate and Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth. They hop aboard a millionaire's yacht, where James cooks a delicious sea bass dish. Paul discovers some fantastic local cheese, while James visits a breeder with mangalista pigs in a beautiful coastline setting. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on some amazing pork and chicken cooked al fresco.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick travels to Bamburgh and tastes some Scotch Pies. After visiting Mike Ainsley, who farms Cheviot sheep, Rick whips up a tasty moussaka, using local lamb.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and his dog Chalky are in the Yorkshire Dales. At Leyburn market, they check out the local dairy produce and, after chatting to the stall-holders, Rick prepares a Yorkshire curd tart.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits John Firth, who rears freshwater crayfish in Galloway, and cooks crayfish with pasta. He then travels to the picturesque town of Ludlow in Shropshire, which is as rich in gastronomic delights as it is in history.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits Cartmel in the Lake District, famous for its ancient 13th century Priory and Jean Johns' sticky toffee pudding. From her village shop in this tiny pretty village, truckloads of puddings are transported daily to all parts of Britain.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits Cork in Southern Ireland. At the English Market, the gastronomic heart of the city, he talks to retailers and cooks corned beef and cabbage. Still in Eire, he travels up the east coast to Waterford, a town usually renowned for its glass.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick is on home turf when he visits the Tintagel area in Cornwall. Here, legend has it, the ruined castle atop the craggy, untamed cliffs is what remains of King Arthur's Camelot.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: In the Clovelly area of North Devon, Rick goes fishing for herring with Steve Perham, whose family have been fishermen for over 200 years. All that fresh sea air gives them a hearty appetite as Rick fries the herring in wholemeal flour.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and Chalky travel to Loch Torridon in Scotland to go fishing with John McGreggor for langoustines. Here the prawns are protected by the Marien Stewardship Council, which has awarded its seal of approval to the fishermen.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick pays a memorable visit to the Channel Island of Jersey, where he goes reef fishing for sea bass, which he then grills. He also makes a consommé and enjoys a beach barbecue.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick takes a moonlit excursion off Newlyn Bay in Cornwall to go pilchard fishing with Nick Howell. Back on the beach, Rick barbecues the sardines and makes a piri piri sauce to accompany them.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue is joined in her kitchen by award-winning musician YolanDa Brown for dishes that include a classic French stew and a family favourite of YolanDa's.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs in and pigs out on all things pork. After sampling a unique take on tacos in San Antonio, he heads to New Orleans to try pork knuckles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is exploring an entire world of barbecue. After sampling smokin' chicken salad in Delaware he heads to Kauai for a traditional Hawaiian pig roast. In San Diego, a real-deal joint with Argentinean flavour.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: An Italian joint sells arancini and veal scaloppini. A street food spot serves shawarma and falafel.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: A true British pub serves authentic English staples. A poke counter dishes out a Hawaiian classic.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.desc: Patrick Christys explore tomorrow's news, tonight, including all the major stories making the front pages and full coverage of all the breaking news at home and abroad.

programme.title: Breakfast with Stephen and Ellie
programme.desc: Start your day with all the news and views from Britain's best Breakfast show.

programme.title: Britain's Newsroom
programme.desc: When the news happens, it happens here. But what does it mean for you? Every weekday morning, Britain's Newsroom brings you the biggest stories and the answers you need from the across the UK.

programme.desc: Every weekday from midday, we bring you the news as it breaks. Wherever it's happening, we'll have it covered - as well as giving our own view and how it matters to you with Tom and Emily.

programme.title: Martin Daubney
programme.desc: Martin gives an up-to-the-minute take on the day's events delivered in his own unique and lively style.

programme.title: Lee Anderson's Real World

programme.title: Friday Night with Matt Goodwin
programme.desc: Britain was once the envy of the world. Join Matt Goodwin as he explores how we fix the problems facing the country.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs - returning contender Alan Murchison and newcomers Colin Buchan and Mark Greenaway. All of them are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. This is the final chance to impress `Great British Menu' veteran Jeremy Lee with their dessert dishes - `Going for Gold', modern day Eton mess, and beetroot cake with chocolate mousse and fennel ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from Scotland. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. Only one chef can make it through to the national finals to represent Scotland. If they win, one of their dishes could be on the menu at The Olympic Feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Central chefs Aktar Islam and Daniel Clifford and newcomer Paul Foster kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be pork neck with ribwort plantain and pork scratchings, veal tartare with caramelised sweetbread and burnt onions or breast of squab with confit leg and chilli and sweetcorn shoots?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Aktar Islam, Daniel Clifford and Paul Foster cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be poached ray wing with chicken skin and sea vegetables, stuffed red mullet with roasted artichoke and parmesan puree, or pickled mackerel with smoked langoustine and infused coconut milk?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Aktar Islam, Daniel Clifford and Paul Foster cook the big one, the main course, but which one will get top marks? programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the final chance for chefs Aktar Islam, Daniel Clifford and Paul Foster to impress Glynn Purnell as they make their desserts - whipped sea buckthorn with meringue and crispy rice, raspberry and tarragon roulade with cookie dough and tarragon oil, and dark chocolate with raspberry jelly and raspberry sorbet. Plus, paralympian Daniel Greaves offers his advice to one chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the Central region. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the judges - Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In this battle of the North East chefs, returning contender Stephanie Moon and newcomers Colin McGurran and Charlie Lakin prepare their respective starters. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contender Stephanie Moon and newcomers Colin McGurran and Charlie Lakin cook their fish course here. Which one is going to be first off the blocks? programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contender Stephanie Moon and newcomers Colin McGurran and Charlie Lakin cook their main course here. Which one will take gold? programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the North East region. One chef left the competition on the previous day and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In the battle of the Northern Ireland chefs - returning contenders Chris Fearon, Chris Bell and Niall McKenna begin with their starters. Which one is going to be first off the blocks? programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Great British Menu

Will it be Chris Bell's roast lissara duck, bakewell tart, cocoa and basil; Niall's dexter beef with miso and fresh almonds; or Chris Fearon's spring jump lamb?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Chris Fearon, Chris Bell and Niall McKenna present their dessert dishes. Will it be Chris Bell's summer salad of jasmine panna cotta, apricots and pistachio cake; Niall's apple sorbet, elderflower custard and whiskey jelly; or Chris Fearon's the torch? Plus, Olympian Amir Khan gives valuable tips on competing at the highest level.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the Northern Ireland region. With two chef's remaining, the must cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan kick things off with their starters. Johnnie makes foie gras ice cream, cherry ravioli and iberico ham; Aiden offers black cherry and foie gras terrine with palm sugar mousse; and Simon's entry is grilled salad, truffle custard, cheese foam and cobnut crisp.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective fish courses. Johnnie concocts a Recreation of the Sea; Aiden makes beetroot poached salmon, caviar, razor clam, citrus and fennel salad; and Simon serves up lobster with pickled beetroot, sweet apple and cuckoo flower.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan are challenged to cook their mains and Marcus Wareing judges the dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective desserts. It's the final chance for the chefs to impress veteran Marcus Wareing as he decides who will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs compete for the chance to cook at a glittering Olympic banquet. The North West finalists go head-to-head as they prepare a lavish meal for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In the battle of the London and South East chefs newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their starter menus. Will it be Phil with spring salad with goat's milk puree, pickled asparagus and quail's eggs; Graham with pickled rabbit, wild garlic quinoa and frozen rabbit liver parfait; or Marcus with iced lamb's liver parfait, malt loaf, fingerling limes and rose geranium?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their fish courses. Will it be Phil with a tasting of Cornish mackerel with oysters, mussels, winkles and samphire; Marcus with pollock, peas, coconut and elderflower; or Graham with octopus, blood orange, feta cheese, squid ink tuile and black olive salt?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness cook their main courses, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Phil with roast loin of lamb with pie and mash, carrots, nettles and mint; Graham with slow cooked marsh lamb breast, rasher and spiced rissole; or Marcus with blade of beef cooked in hay, tendons and beetroot?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their dessert dishes here. Will it be Phil with rhubarb and custard souffle, Marcus with asparagus, goat's curd and black olive, or Graham with lemon meringue? Veteran Jason Atherton decides who goes through to the judges and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from the London and South East region. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. The chef's task is to create a menu which captures the Olympic spirit.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the battle of the Wales chefs: returning contenders Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry. All are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. Stephen Terry cooks `The Opening Ceremony', Richard Davies makes ravioli of quail, parfait of foie gras, roasted salsify and Madeira jelly, and James Sommerin cooks sage cream, onion, chicken and Welsh `Brie' truffle toast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry are all determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. They cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Fish and Shellfish Medley', Richard Davies with compression of vine tomato, poached lobster and hand dived scallop, or James Sommerin with lobster and Iberico ham, spiced butter and broccoli?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their main course, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Bunny Pentathlon', Richard Davies with slow cooked rib of beef, beetroot ketchup, bone marrow and braised shallot, or James Sommerin with mangalitsa pork, carrot, liquorice and leek?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their dessert, but who will impress? Stephen Terry with `Bronze, Silver or Gold?', Richard Davies with passion fruit cream and gel with bitter chocolate sorbet or James Sommerin with raspberry and lemon cheesecake? `Great British Menu' veteran Angela Hartnett decides who goes through to the judges on Friday and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from Wales. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: South West contenders Paul Ainsworth and Nathan Outlaw, plus newcomer Simon Hulstone, kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be Paul with his `Breakfast of Champions'; Nathan with hogs pudding with seaweed, potato terrine and mushroom ketchup; or Simon Hulstone with track and field - beetroot and goats cheese?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish course, but which one but which one will prevail? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with monkfish `Two Showings'; Nathan Outlaw with mackerel, horseradish and oyster sauce; or Simon Hulstone with `The Swimming Pool'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course, but which chef will take gold? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Wishful Chicken'; Nathan Outlaw with duck, barbecue monkfish, rosemary, samphire and asparagus; or Simon Hulstone with lamb with salted toffee pea mousse, shallot puree and asparagus?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chefs' final chance to impress the veteran with their dessert dishes. Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Then and Now'; Nathan Outlaw with elderflower and lemon tart, with strawberry sorbet and meringues; or Simon Hulstone with `Closing Ceremony'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the South West region. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The 2012 competition enters its final week. programme.title: Hitler's Lost Battles
programme.desc: The poor military decisions made by Hitler started to become clear by 1942. America got involved in the conflict as a reaction to his declaration of war, as they made ground in North Africa.

programme.title: Great American Generals - Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, Bradley
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage provides insight into what made Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, and Bradley heroes of American history.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.sub-title: The Jew Who Fought for Hitler
programme.desc: The 1935 Nuremburg Laws laid the groundwork for what would evolve into The Final Solution.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Anton Mussert was one of the founders of the National Socialist Movement in the Netherlands and worked with the Gestapo.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Greece had a well integrated Jewish community and lost 300,000 citizens thanks to Nazi occupiers; many Greeks collaborated with the Nazis.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Scrutinising the motives behind the individuals and groups who aided the Nazi war effort. Respected mathematician Rolf Nevanlinna led the Finnish SS against Stalin's Russia.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: The Schutzmannschaft were the collaborationist auxiliary police forces of Nazi-occupied countries in Eastern Europe.

programme.title: Remember Pearl Harbor Narrated by Tom Selleck
programme.desc: On December 7, 1941, Japanese planes dropped torpedoes and bombs on an American Pacific fleet at Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: The ongoing battles in Manchuria, China, Abyssinia, and Spain each serve as a terrible precedent in their own way.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: As the war consumes economic, agricultural and industrial resources, the extent of the war is realised across Europe, Asia and the Pacific.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how people and resources became absorbed in the war efforts by occupying forces during WWII.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how civilians became defenders against targets of aerial warfare, and the strategic bombing campaigns utilised in an attempt to end the war.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: The cloak of war played a role in the genocide of WWII, allowing unfathomable horrors against innocent women, children, and men. Even within Allied nations, individuals were seen as a potential enemies and denied liberty.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores the aftermath of WWII in the wake of destruction and liberation, with tens of millions dead and millions more displaced.

programme.title: Wings: World War Two in the Skies
programme.desc: In the Second World War, a new generation of planes and daredevil pilots take aerial combat to deadly new heights.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: In 12th-century Ireland, provincial kings vie for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henry's vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland with frightening power.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Following the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171, Henry II himself arrives to takes control of Ireland, and grants land there to settlers from Britain. Now, archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: This first episode takes Dan behind the battlements of Dover Castle, where he descends into an underground lair to discover how a vast network of tunnels has been used to defend both the castle and the country for centuries.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comedian Loyiso Gola embark on a road trip through South Africa to explore the UK's imperial history in the country, from colonial wars to the deals that eventually led to the development of apartheid.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al and comic Kevin Kropinyeri reveal that Captain James Cook was not really a captain, explore the Aboriginal history of Australian cricket, and uncover the often brutal land grabs that impacted Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: Pompeii - The Last Mysteries Revealed
programme.desc: Enjoy a journey through time, joining archaeologists as they tread new ground and search for new secrets in one of the world's most fascinating places - Pompeii. (1 of 2)

programme.title: Pompeii - The Last Mysteries Revealed
programme.desc: Archaeologists continue their exploration of Pompeii, uncovering the secrets of this fascinating ancient city, with surprises to be found and trouble ahead.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019's most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the North, the remains of a Tudor house in Leicestershire were thought to be the childhood home of England's forgotten Queen, Lady Jane Grey. But when archaeologists excavate, they find more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: This first episode takes Dan behind the battlements of Dover Castle, where he descends into an underground lair to discover how a vast network of tunnels has been used to defend both the castle and the country for centuries.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Built by William the Conqueror, the Tower of London is one of Britain's most iconic landmarks. It has been a military fortress, a palace, a royal mint, a prison, a zoo and a place of execution, a silent witness to some of the most momentous events in our history. Dan Jones reveals some of its fascinating secrets and tells the stories of the traitors, rebels and royalty who met their end inside its imposing walls.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Edwardian Britain by visiting Cardiff, which grew rich selling the coal that powered the Empire. Alice hears the stories of the Edwardian miners and their backbreaking and often dangerous work.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the English Civil War through the history of Oxford, the city at the heart of a battle between an autocratic king and his rebellious parliament.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britain's beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the world's industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. Tony Robinson tracks their 50-mile walk from Penrith past Ullswater to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Celts: The Untold Story
programme.desc: A journey to some of the mysteries of how the early Celts emerged and expanded, from collapsed salt mines, to bizarre rituals and lost cities.

programme.title: Scary Tales of New York

programme.title: Scary Tales of New York

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson is out to discover why Pembrokeshire feels so English. There are castles everywhere and it boasts one of the country's finest cathedrals, sure signs that the Normans stamped their mark all over this little slice of Wales.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony's walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the county's hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tony's journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019's most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An enormous semi in Rotherham, an impressive former chapel in Fraddon in Cornwall and a tiny terraced house in Putney in London go under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a one-bedroom flat next to a train line in London with a hefty guide price, while Martin meets a father and son team working on a two-bedroom terrace in Abertillary. He also visits a pair of semis in Walderslade in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a primary school teacher with a basement flat in East Dulwich in London, while Martin meets property developers in Ebbw Vale in Wales and Workington in Cumbria - both of whom have ambitious plans for kitchens and bathrooms.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It's a family affair for all three property projects in Port Talbot, Wimbledon and Telford. A father and daughter, cousins and a father and his two stepsons tackle projects bought at auction from just over £20,000 to more than £1.2 million.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy enjoys a day by the seaside visiting a two bedroom maisonette a few steps from the water in Kent's Leysdown-on-Sea, while Martin has a look at a terraced house in Cymmer in the Welsh Valleys and a two bedroom mid-terrace in Penkhull near Stoke.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks around a terraced house in West Bromwich and another in Telford, while Lucy checks on progress 18-months after she first visited a bungalow on a large plot near Gillingham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin visits a two-bedroom flat in Budleigh Salterton and a three-bed terrace in Downpatrick, Northern Ireland, which needs a new lease of life. Lucy meets the buyer of a plot of land in Abbey Wood, London, who has big plans for its future.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy catches up with a developer she met three years ago. This time he has an ambitious project turning two flats in a Victorian house in Hounslow into three. Martin meets two first-time developers working on projects in Glasgow and Tipton.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Edinburgh, a 1930s terraced house packed full with original features in Bromley in Kent and a five-bedroom house in Exeter in danger of falling down are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A smart looking terrace with an expensive problem to fix in Pontygwaith in Wales, a large Victorian house in Okehampton in Devon and an old pub in Sittingbourne in Kent all go under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin visits a listed shop in Hodnet in Shropshire and a property developer working on a maisonette in Ferndale in South Wales. Lucy follows a five-year project to restore a 17th-century listed building in Margate in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy go flat-tastic looking around buildings which developers hope will create around a dozen flats. The properties sold under the hammer are in Stoke in Staffordshire, Bexleyheath in Kent and Gunnislake in Cornwall.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A five-bedroom house in Plymouth, a two-bed Victorian cottage in Croydon and a former coach house in Cardiff are sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the developers to hear about their plans to make the most of their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The trio look at a family home in Kent, a former bar in Llanelli in Wales and a terrace in Croydon, all sold at auction. Will any of the developers make a return on their investments?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi in Crewe, a top-floor flat in London and a terraced house in Oldham are sold under the hammer. All the owners are happy with the prices they paid, but will any of them be able to turn a profit at the end of the projects?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: While developers in Croydon in Surrey and Donnington in Shropshire wrestle with the question of whether to lose a bedroom each for a better placed kitchen or bathroom, two friends in Derby consider creating two more in their six-bedroom house.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers buy a rundown cottage in Probus in Cornwall, a father buys a flat in Mitcham in Surrey as a long-term investment for his young daughter and a seasoned property developer buys a house in Tunstall in Stoke-on-Trent for her daughter.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Plaistow in London looking around a three-bedroom semi, Lucy is checking out a small piece of land in Crayford in Kent, and Martin meets a father and son who have bought a small house with a big garden in Wheelock in Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former miner's cottage in Aberaman in South Wales and semis in Adderley Green in Stoke and Streatham in South London are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion look around auction buys in Birmingham, Oldham and Gravesend and discover what the new owners plan to do with their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion travel the country meeting developers hoping to make some money from their auction buys. Today they are in Stoke, Croydon and Warrington hearing tales from the auction rooms.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former doctor's surgery in need of TLC is converted into a family home in Houghton-le-spring and a flat in a 1930s block of flats in Edgware and a detached house in a woodland garden in Ashford in Kent are also given makeovers.

programme.title: Dermot's Taste of Ireland
programme.desc: Dermot heads to the wild west of Donegal to explore the county's Irish-speaking Gaeltacht, where the people, music and food are anything but ordinary. After a crash course at Glencolmcille Folk Village - a living museum celebrating old Irish traditions - Dermot bravely puts his Irish to the test ordering a pint in the local pub. He also discovers an old Irish cure-all: carrageen moss pudding, a surprisingly delicious seaweed dessert said to keep everything from colds to heartbreak at bay.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a magical trip to the stunning Isle of Skye, where he's joined by top chef and ultimate Scotsman Tony Singh. Tony visits a local bakery which doubles as an art gallery and learns to make the local oatcakes. James is inspired by the amazing local seafood and cooks up a tasty dish using plaice and lovage. Their day comes to a perfect end aboard a boat, where they spot dozens of dolphins, and James cooks up a fresh lobster and langoustine dish with an added Scottish twist.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When Stella's more famous sister returns after a 40-year family rift, the killings escalate, each of them reminiscent of a movie plot, is life - and death - imitating art?

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britain's best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: John contemplates moving away with Fiz to escape the unwanted attention of her friends and Maria puts a stop to Liam going on a business trip to New York.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: With Fiz refusing to speak to him, John sees red when he collects a flirtatious Rosie from town in his cab. Liam asks Carla to meet him and Janice keeps quiet about a lottery win.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Accusations fly as Maggie is revealed to be the main beneficiary of a patient's will, and Greengrass has a talented visitor.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordly underestimates the strength of local feeling about access to a footpath, and Greengrass becomes a chimney sweeper.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is leaving for Spain, Bert and Jennifer's engagement party goes off with a bang and Al is hailed as a hero for saving Caroline's life.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The drama about a curmudgeonly country doctor and his patients in Cornwall returns for a seventh series. Martin returns to Portwenn, but without Louisa. She has gone to stay with her mother in Spain to take some time out to think, and Martin is left wondering if she will ever come back. Aunt Ruth has found him a therapist, but life in Portwenn continues to get in the doc's way - in the form of Steve Baker and his lifeboat training exercise.

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When the body of a missing ten-year-old schoolgirl is found, DS Ronnie Brooks and DS Matt Devlin investigate an emotionally-charged child abduction case. However, when the case takes a shocking and unexpected twist, Crown Prosecutors James Steel and Alesha Phillips are forced to face some very tough choices in pursuit of justice.

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: DS Brooks, DS Devlin and DI Chandler investigate when a homeless man is found beaten nearly to death in the park at the centre of a well-kept square. As they dig deeper, they discover that the man tormented some of the local residents, giving several people a motive. A respectable resident of the square is arrested, but Senior Crown Prosecutor James Steel fears that the jury will sympathise with the alleged attacker.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: In a police nursing home, Wycliffe ponders the future after the attempt on his life. Meanwhile, Kersey investigates a salmon poacher's suspicious death, and acting DCI Lane is tempted to move on and up.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The wife of one of Kersey's friends goes missing after an evening class and later her tutor is found murdered. Meanwhile, Kersey's relationship with Lane threatens to lead him into deep waters.

programme.title: Boon

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two young men are involved in a robbery and there's Flying Squad insider dealings concerning Det Chief Inspector Haskins. However, what's the connection and can Regan crack it before it's too late?

programme.title: BattleBots
programme.desc: It's the last chance saloon for some teams tonight, in their final qualifying round before the World Championship; some are out to win a coveted top tournament seed, while others just want to survive; for everyone, it's do or die.

programme.title: Magnum, P.I.
programme.desc: When an auspicious actor is shot with a supposedly fake gun, stunt double Magnum is on hand to investigate. Was this an awful accident or a callous crime?

programme.title: Magnum, P.I.
programme.desc: A Vietnam vet has deadly plans for a former foe.

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When the Saint finds a girl determined to kill a man, he steps right into her life to find out why - and ends up organising a revolution in his own inimitable style.

programme.title: Boon
programme.desc: Ken and Margaret are used to provide a false alibi and find themselves under suspicion by the police.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Barry Tyson and Peter Jenner arrive at their lock-up with £27,000 they've just stolen. Suddenly a pistol is pointed at them and a fight ensues, Jenner is hit and the intruder escapes with the money. Can the Flying Squad catch the gunmen and will Regan take the security job offer?

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits Australia's Fitzroy River in search of one of the worlds rarest and least known fish: the Northern River Shark. It's so elusive that only 20 have been caught.

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the fourth weekend of the 2026 British Touring Car Championship from the Oulton Park circuit in Cheshire. Nicki Shields presents the best of the action from rounds 10, 11 and 12, with race commentary coming from David Addison and Tim Harvey.

programme.title: Skyscraper
programme.desc: Will Sawyer is a former FBI agent and U.S. war veteran who now assesses security for skyscrapers. While he's on assignment in China, the world's tallest and safest building catches on fire -- and Will gets framed for it. Now a wanted man and on the run, he must find those responsible, clear his name and somehow rescue his family members when they become trapped inside the inferno.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes's older brother, Mycroft, needs help solving the mystery of a young Greek forced by a gang to interpret for a man they are holding prisoner until he signs an important document.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A young solicitor is arrested moments after presenting a knotty problem concerning the death of Jonas Oldacre, an eccentric builder who has disappeared after making out a will in the solicitor's favour.

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Sharpe is entrusted with preparing the Royal Irish Company for battle, a company that has previously only performed ceremonial duties for the Spanish King.

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: On a mission to protect the identity of Wellington's master spy El Mirador, the dashing swordsman is nearly done for by a French foe. Nursed back to health, Sharpe continues his search for the man who injured him.

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Sharpe and Harper return to England to recruit for the South Essex regiment and discover corruption in the highest ranks of the British army. Reporting their findings only makes them targets in an assassination attempt, so to prove their allegations they fake their own deaths and re-enlist in the South Essex.

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Sharpe marries his sweetheart Jane but, before the honeymoon is over, has to leave on a perilous mission to capture a French fort high in the Pyrenees. While he is away, Jane catches a deadly fever which is sweeping through the British camp.

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The hunters research the estate of Southampton market trader worth over £100,000. Meanwhile, another firm's investigation reveals a family's historical link to the very beginnings of professional sport.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Norfolk. Simon O'Brien discovers an old pedal cycle with a difference - it's got a two-stroke engine attached - while Henry Cole finds a side table made from a war shell case.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first to host are husband-and-wife team Paul Billington and Flora Loizou from Silverwood Lodge in Cambridgeshire, the only couple in this week's competition. As the hosts show their three female guests to their rooms, Caroline, owner of The Dean Bed & Breakfast, gives a frosty response to the fact that they don't live on site, while Sue from Seashells Guesthouse is left in hysterics.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group are off to Great Yarmouth to stay in Sue's dog friendly Seashells Guesthouse. After revealing that she allows dogs to sleep in the beds during the last B&B visit, no one knows quite what to expect. As soon as they pull up, all the guests are blown away by the doggy-themed exterior. The interior is equally memorable, with Paul and Flora finding themselves in the Cappuccino Room and Caroline in the Poppy Room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: After making her feelings known at the first two B&B visits, American ex-banker Nancy Hampson isn't everybody's favourite person. As the third host, she's attempting to put all that behind her, but there's trouble from the off at The Old Court Guesthouse. Paul and Flora are unhappy with the size of the bed they've been given, so Nancy offers to move them.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to Scotland to stay with final host Caroline at The Dean Bed and Breakfast. The picturesque house, its two acres of gardens and breath-taking views across the Firth of Forth immediately win everyone over. However, rogue underwear in the drawers and cobwebs on the curtains are touches that the guests aren't so keen on.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: There are tensions, tempers and tears as the group reconvene for one last time to thrash out the feedback, reveal the payments and announce the winner in the competition to be named best value B&B of the week. Paul and Flora from Silverwood Lodge are fired up about Nancy's comments and a tussle over TVs and hot water leads to accusations of lying and game playing.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition is in Edinburgh, where the first host is 37-year-old Canadian comedian Larah who hopes to bag herself the prize with a night of fine dining.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the second night from the competition in Edinburgh, and cafe owner Nutan is hoping her happy-go-lucky attitude will have her laughing all the way to the bank.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition is in Edinburgh and next host is South African born family man Sindiso. Sindiso is hoping his night of South African fun will win him the prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition is in Edinburgh, and up hosting is security company manager, Anne. Anne is hoping that her homely, relaxed night in will be enough for her to win the £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night in Edinburgh, and pilot James is hoping a touch of sophistication in his high flying menu will see him land safely in first place. But after last night's tension, his main concern is keeping any potential explosions contained.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Karen and her son Liam welcome their rivals to Jockhedge Holiday Cottages in Burgh le Marsh, Lincolnshire, where luxury awaits. But not everything lives up to the high-end price tag.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Carolynne and her son George have high hopes for their brand-new BandB, The Victoria Inn in Witton-le-Wear, County Durham. But one guest gets locked in the loo and breakfast gets a bashing.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Aidan and Mij host at the Earlston House Hotel in Paignton, Devon. One guest gets a bum deal with the bog roll and, at breakfast, there's kitchen confusion and sausage snobbery.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Pete Lacey's Beach House in Portland, Dorset. His back-to-basics BandB doesn't do it for everyone, but it's Karen and Liam's feedback that really gets Pete's goat.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the last day of the competition. After dramatic discoveries and score-settling on everything from losing sleep to smelly showers, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer travel to Northern Ireland. Phil househunts in Bangor for Lynn, who's been blind since birth, and in Belfast, Kirstie seeks a place to share for two busy doctors.

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Broadcaster Nikki Fox, choirmaster and presenter Gareth Malone, TV presenter Jean Johansson, comedian Nish Kumar and pop royalty Claire Richards take on the challenge with host Ken Bruce. Find out who'll be top of the pops.

programme.title: The Sommerdahl Murders
programme.desc: The fourth series of the crime drama from Walter Presents. Sexual shenanigans and murder are a potent mix in Dan and Flemming's latest case.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Natai is rushed to A&E with a lacerated wrist after putting his hand through a glass pane. Pauline is struggling to breathe. And, 17-year-old Fahad has crippling stomach pain.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A cyclist is rushed to hospital with head trauma after he's hit by a car. A 92-year-old falls when out walking near his home. An injured painter-decorator talks about being a dad.

programme.desc: Paramedics rush to treat a diabetic woman involved in a car crash, while an 82-year-old with breathing difficulties is close to death and a man suffers chest pains at work.

programme.title: Teen Mom OG
programme.desc: For four seasons fans of `Teen Mom' watched Farrah, Maci, Amber and Catelynn face multiple challenges in their first years of motherhood, including such things as graduating from high school, relationships, getting jobs, beginning college and moving out on their own. Oh, and raising their first child. Now the original girls - hence, the `OG' in the revived series title - return to reveal the challenges of raising children on camera, and how life in the public eye has shaped them as parents and people. It's an unprecedented view of young mothers facing the responsibilities of parenthood as their children enter kindergarten, start little league and reach other milestones of youth.

programme.desc: Frances takes the stage full of energy to deliver her silly physical humour.

programme.title: Zoltan Kaszas: Cat Jokes
programme.desc: Hungarian-born Zoltan Kaszas leans in about growing up in San Diego and slew of cat jokes in a stand-up special.

programme.title: Jason Hewlett: Father Time
programme.desc: The award-winning one-man show of Jason Hewlett The Entertainer will have the viewer laughing until it hurts. Comedy, music, impressions, and standing vvations make Jason the perfect entertainer.

programme.title: Greg Warren: Fish Sandwich
programme.desc: Greg Warren is a nationally touring headliner and has been featured on "Late Night with Seth Meyers", "Last Comic Standing", "Comedy Central Presents", "The Late Late Show with Craig Ferguson", BET and CMT.

programme.title: Larry Omaha: Freaky Times in a Freaky World
programme.desc: Comedian Larry Omaha offers a fresh perspective on growing up indigenous on the plains, the Washington Redskins, and working as a shoeshine boy.

programme.title: Jay Whittaker: Safe for Work
programme.desc: Jay Whittaker is a comedian, actor, host and writer hailing from Los Angeles and Salt Lake City.

programme.title: Brad Upton: Will Be Funny for Money
programme.desc: Brad Upton first stepped on stage in September of 1984 and he's never looked back. This ex-grade school teacher is now in his 30th year of comedy and is nearing 6000 lifetime performances.

programme.title: Jeff Allen: Honor Thy Wife
programme.desc: Jeff Allen, an entertainer, presents a hilarious stand-up comedy act focused on married life.

programme.title: Ty Barnett: Yep...
programme.desc: Comedian Ty Barnett performs.

programme.title: Jonnie W: Not My Cup of Tea
programme.desc: Jonnie discusses new country radio, double decker grave plots, and the difference between fish and beef.

programme.title: Tim Cavanagh: The Joys of Uncle Dumb
programme.desc: With a companion guitar, Tim Cavanagh asks for a wake up call, points out another unhappy marriage, finds a human music stand, and plays a possum game.

programme.title: Susan Jones: Lady Ha Ha
programme.desc: Susan Jones is a tenacious headlining comedian, writer, master of ceremonies, and motivational force with national touring experience.

programme.title: Brandon Young: Chicken Gone
programme.desc: Brandon Young has 1000 words for any picture and a shadow that sets off automatic doors.

programme.title: Rob Little: Ado-Rob-Ble
programme.desc: Rob Little's outlook on life can be summed up in two words: Pure optimism. In a recent interview he revealed, in elementary school, kids would always pull food away from him at lunchtime saying mean, hurtful things like "Let go of my sandwich!".

programme.title: Mary Mack: All Ages
programme.desc: Folk Humorist Mary Mack performs her sometimes-musical comedy at festivals, colleges, clubs, casinos and anywhere one can find a llama or a buffet line.

programme.title: Dan St. Paul: Mocking the Suburbs
programme.desc: With thirty years of experience, Dan St. Paul performs an evening of standup comedy about turning fifty, the way his body has changed over the years and the idiosyncrasies of suburban life.

programme.title: Joe Zimmerman: Captain Snuggle Pants
programme.desc: Joe Zimmerman grew up in Morgantown, West Virginia, and started his comedy career in North Carolina. While living in the South, his stand-up group the Beards of Comedy landed a record deal with Comedy Central Records.

programme.title: Tim Cavanagh: The Joys of Uncle Dumb
programme.desc: With a companion guitar, Tim Cavanagh asks for a wake up call, points out another unhappy marriage, finds a human music stand, and plays a possum game.

programme.title: Ali Brice: Bin Wondering
programme.desc: A master of the surrealist arts, Ali Brice takes the audience on a journey through his mind at a time when he spent several hours in a wheelie bin.

programme.title: Njambi McGrath: Breaking Black
programme.desc: Njambi McGrath offers acerbic anecdotes about life in Kenya, living in a white neighbourhood, why immigrants are attracted to the UK, and how they thrive amongst certain hostilities.

programme.title: Ted Hill: All the Presidents Man
programme.desc: From Washington to Biden, Ted talks about his recovery from mental breakdown and includes a joke about each and every US president. Featuring silly graphs, silly drawings, silly jokes, and more.

programme.title: President Obonjo - Stolen
programme.desc: President Obonjo the exiled dictator fights back after an attempted coup by the West. With his army of comedians, he garners support to reinstate himself as the original African dictator.

programme.title: Lloyd Langford: Rascal
programme.desc: A glimpse into the private musings of a man who takes pleasure everywhere he can find it, from the weather and terrorism, to what to do if you find a box of sex toys in your hotel room.

programme.title: Andy Field: The Andy Field Experience
programme.desc: Andy Field puts on a traditional, gag-lead stand-up show with his own iconic charm.

programme.title: Dylan Brody: Driving Hollywood
programme.desc: Brody explores his experiences of heartbreak and hope, and recalls a lifetime of professional disappointment and disillusionment on the drive to and from what might just be the most important meeting of his career.

programme.title: Darius Davies: Don't Be SH:1T
programme.desc: Darius Davies performs an 'all killer no filler. Guaranteed laughs.' special.

programme.desc: Fog moves in while the neighbours are enjoying a boat trip. Vanessa the dragon rushes back to switch on her lighthouse but it fails to shine. She enlists Duster the dog to find a new way to guide her friends home.

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team head to the South Devon Railway to explore the life of the branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Ruth hand-milks a local breed of cow, and discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle. After undergoing an eyesight test, Victorian-style; Peter joins the footplate crew on the South Devon line. But it is not all plain-sailing when it comes to driving the milk train through the night.

programme.title: Ewan and Colin McGregor: RAF Centenary
programme.desc: Ewan and his brother Colin McGregor explore the first hundred years of the RAF's service through the heroic, adventurous and moving stories of the airmen and women, all part of the history of the world's first independent air force.

programme.title: Bombshell
programme.desc: Bombshell explores how the U.S. government manipulated the narrative about the human impact of the bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki. (S)

programme.title: Prisoner Number A26188: Henia Bryer
programme.desc: Charting one woman's survival through the genocidal horrors of the Second World War. Born into a middle class Jewish family in Poland, Henia lost her father, brother and sister during the German occupation, and survived four concentration camps before going on to bear witness to the creation of Israel in 1948.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Londoner Pablo Henderson is first to arrive, with his late father's defunct record turntable. His father arrived in the UK from Jamaica in the 1960s and loved music.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they make wind turbines with twice the wingspan of a Boeing? Plus, how do they mould car tyres from the ooze of a tree?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they make the tank piercing super bullet? Plus, how do they forge the infamous torture weapon, the dentist's drill?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they make giant floating power stations that provide power around the world? Plus, how do they fabricate the world's smallest aircraft?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they create coloured crayons used by children and artists around the world? Plus, how do artisans and craftsmen create treasured mirrors in India?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The Mazda RX7 is an 80s classic loved on both sides of the Atlantic. Mike finds a desirable 1985 MK1 with dodgy aftermarket add-ons and a dubious rotary engine.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike Brewer and Ant Anstead work together to give new life to run-down classic automobiles to resell them at a profit. Working on a tight budget, Mike must find a vehicle for Ant to fix, but that isn't always the easiest task.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. Today, the pair visit two amazing sets of sheds in the Oxfordshire and Hampshire area. In the heart of Oxfordshire they find rare Minis, 1970s Rollers, and a pristine rally car. The Hampshire sheds are full to the rafters with Japanese bikes, but they come away with a real surprise. Selling their projects on, however, proves to be a real challenge.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. Today, on the Cornish coast, Henry and Sam spend the day surrounded by vintage vehicles with genteel pedigrees including a the first ever Lotus Elan, and a pre-war Sunbeam. In Oxfordshire, Sam falls for a a Midget racer - but the restoration is a daunting task.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Follow the amazing manufacturing processes behind alligator bags, lockers, bench planes and deployable flight recorders.

programme.title: How It's Made

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories

programme.title: Married to Evil
programme.desc: A successful Florida family has their American dream shattered when a mysterious figure dressed as a clown shoots a loving mother on her doorstep. The case goes cold for decades until an unexpected break helps bring the killer clown to justice.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: When Tyarra Williams vanishes, tips suggest she may have fallen prey to human trafficking. It is possible some in the community could know more than they are letting on about her disappearance.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In 2019, after a night out with friends, college student Samantha Josephson climbs into a car she mistakes for her rideshare, never to be seen alive again. With little to go on, police must rely on CCTV footage to help pick up the killer's trail.

programme.title: The Detectives: My Killer Case

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: Nikki Whitehead, a mother of two, is brutally killed in her own home. Police think she may have known her killer. Polygraphs and CCTV footage help reveal the terrible truth.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Surviving a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Two women survive violent attacks by a killer, but it's not until a third woman survives that the victims finally attain justice.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: A parolee dodges prison by stealing the identity of a murder victim. A suspect conceals a crime by assuming the victim's identity.

programme.title: On the Case With Paula Zahn

programme.title: Gangster No. 1: The Freddie Foreman Story
programme.desc: Attached to almost every unsolved gangland murder in London over the last 50 years, Freddie Foreman is the `Godfather' of Britain's criminal underworld. Foreman reveals the audacious details of the heists, double crossings, shootouts, murders and betrayals he was involved in, and ultimately, what has made him `Gangster Number.1'.

programme.title: The Unperfect Crime
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the 1997 Loomis-Fargo armoured car heist in North Carolina and the serio-comic fates of its underdog perpetrators. who fell victim to avarice, mutual betrayals and false romantic promises.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: The death of Peppino Impastato in 1978 was dismissed by police as a random act. But he was an anti-Mafia activist. Were the people behind his death sheltered by the Mafia?

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Seemingly normal couple Karla Hornolka and Paul Bernardo murdered two teenage girls together, but would their dedication to one another last through the trial?

programme.title: Couples Who Kill
programme.desc: A disgruntled pizza restaurant employee seeks revenge on his colleagues by going on a murderous rampage.

programme.title: Couples Who Kill
programme.desc: An elderly Texas couple find themselves in danger when they invite some deadly guests into their home.

programme.title: Couples Who Kill
programme.desc: Delving inside criminal minds and exploring the roots of more deadly duos, this series investigates some of the most terrifying crimes in recent history.

programme.title: Wayne Couzens: Killer in Plain Sight
programme.desc: This extraordinary film reveals the shocking truth about the serving police officer who killed Sarah Everard, and asks how he was able to hide from scrutiny for so long.

programme.title: The Murder Network: A Serial Killer in Nazi Paris
programme.desc: In France, 1942, a serial killer stalks the streets of occupied Paris. Using the chaos of the war to his own advantage, psychotic doctor Marcel Petiot poses as a resistance operative and preys on those fleeing the nazis.

programme.title: The Unsolved Killings of Jack the Ripper
programme.desc: Looking into the different theories about who was behind the brutal Whitechapel killings that rocked East London in 1888.

programme.title: The Boneyard
programme.desc: Chilling documentary charting a brutal real life tale of kidnapping, enslavement and murder. Find out how investigators tracked down a malicious murderer and explore the extensive trial that followed.

programme.title: Couples Who Kill
programme.desc: Masked robbers burst into a bachelor party, guns ablaze. Two men are killed and others seriously injured. Can police find the murderers before they kill again?

programme.title: Couples Who Kill
programme.desc: Explore the twisted minds of Phillip and Nancy Garrido who kidnapped 11-year-old Jaycee Dugard in 1991, and sexually and emotionally abused her for 18 years.

programme.title: Killed Walking Home
programme.desc: Every three days, a woman is killed in the UK. These women could be sisters, daughters and friends. Although there is a lead that breaks open the case, what comes in light of the investigation is one that leaves an everlasting scar on the families.

programme.title: When Fred Met Rose
programme.desc: Documentary series examining what led Fred and Rose West to become serial killers.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Focuses on the notorious house which the couple shared in Gloucester. Fred and Rose murdered their first three victims - Anna McFall, Charmaine West and then Rena Costello - because they `got in the way'. But when they moved into 25 Cromwell Street, the couple began to kill for sexual thrills.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Looks at how a dogged police officer, Hazel Savage, fought to have the Wests' patio excavated when she became suspicious about the whereabouts of their daughter. The film also includes insights from both Fred and Rose West's former solicitors.

programme.title: Fred & Rose West: Love & Murder
programme.desc: Fred and Rose West raped, tortured and murdered at least a dozen young British girls between 1967 and 1987, including several of their own daughters. Learn how two of Britain's most notorious serial killers were able to get away with their crimes for so long, how they hid the victim's remains within the confines of their own home, and how they were eventually taken down.

programme.title: Myra Hindley: The Untold Story
programme.desc: Exploring Hindley's childhood and the start of her relationship with Brady.

programme.title: Myra Hindley: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The stories of the victims and those who got away.

programme.title: Myra Hindley: The Untold Story
programme.desc: A look at the life and crimes of infamous killer Myra Hindley. This film explores the stories of Brady and Hindley's five known murder victims and the accounts of the children who got away, most notably David Smith.

programme.title: Coral Sea Dreaming
programme.desc: The filmmaker highlights the survival strategies of marine creatures in a coral, which is an underwater ecosystem characterised by reef-building.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Colouring that matches or blends with their surroundings helps the Arctic fox, green tree frog and penguin hide from predators.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Many animals such as toads, moths, snakes and spiders have poison glands, while the spines of the stone fish and the scorpion fish are poisonous.

programme.title: Lionfish: The New Pirates of the Caribbean
programme.desc: Armed with 18 venomous spines, the lionfish hunts, invisible to its prey, in packs. Shedding light on the fascinating behaviour that let the lionfish conquer foreign waters, and by doing so threaten entire ecosystems.

programme.title: Lions: Africa's Super Power
programme.desc: The endless plains of East Africa is a paradise for lions. The most social of all cats, lions form prides that are unlike any other animals. Together, they'll hunt, raise their young and defend their territory.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Learn about four different regions of Switzerland. From the rugged mountains of the Ticino High Alps to the lovely Muggio Valley in the southernmost corner of Ticino, hardly any other canton is so full of contrasts.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The river called Aare is the only body of water that runs through Switzerland. It rises from a source in the Bernese Alps and finally flows into the Rhine. The Aare offers important habitat for flora and fauna.

programme.title: Addo's Elephants
programme.desc: Savannah elephants thrive in South Africa's Addo Elephant National Park.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: The archer fish spits water from a `blowgun' in its mouth to capture bugs, while the jawfish spits pebbles from its mouth to defend itself.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Cuttlefish and squid eject a black fluid to help them hide from predators, while skunks aren't the only animals to use scent glands for defence.

programme.title: Vicious Beauties: The Secret World of Jellyfish
programme.desc: Jellyfish mature from polyp to medusa; few survive, yet some coasts see blooms and long-lived adults.

programme.title: Wild Galapagos
programme.sub-title: In the Grip of the Ocean
programme.desc: The reef life, and the birds, lizards and reptiles that cope with the lava rock islands of the Galapagos, make this remote series of islands a unique natural habitat.

programme.title: Wild Galapagos
programme.desc: The Galapagos Islands are home to animals that have made adaptations, including penguins, iguanas, tortoises, cormorants and - possibly the oddest - the vampire finch.

programme.title: Baboon Bandits
programme.desc: A close look at the Table Mountain baboons and the local reactions they incite.

programme.desc: The Congo River, Africa's second largest, grows from Zambia, gaining strength as it flows westward.

programme.title: Myths of the Congo

programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. programme.desc: Paula Stillie has always looked completely different from her white adoptive family in Scotland. programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Cantering towards an auction in Newmarket, Natasha Raskin and Paul Laidlaw are taking their classic car around Norfolk and Suffolk on an antiques hunt. The experts rummage far and wide, unearthing such objects as Ottoman militaria and a vital bit of old train.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Natasha Raskin are antiquing in Norfolk, but somehow finding time to go down a Neolithic flint mine and go into print. It's the final `Round Britain Rummage' and they unearth a rarely seen art nouveau food warmer and a charming 19th-century table croquet set.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Interior designer Sarah Moore meets people up and down the UK who are throwing out what they consider to be worthless goods. She takes three of these worn-out items and transforms them beyond recognition, which in turn gives them a higher value.

From then on Hippo stayed in the cool waters without a single hair on her hide.

programme.title: The Running Man
programme.desc: Desperate for money to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards joins the top-rated show on television, "The Running Man," a deadly competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins.

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.desc: The wife of a dentist convinces him to betray their new neighbour - a hit man turned informant - to the Chicago gangsters who want him dead. In need of money in order to hurry through a divorce, the young man arranges a meeting with the Mob in which he will exchange information for cash, but when his scheming wife tells the hit man what he is up to, it is the dentist's life that is placed in grave danger.

programme.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy
programme.desc: Knockabout comedy set in the sexist world of 1970s newscasting. Ron Burgundy, San Diego's top-rated anchorman, reigns supreme in the all-male world of television news, until the arrival of an ambitious female reporter upsets the status quo. When she steps in to present the programme in Ron's absence, an uncivil war breaks out in the newsroom.

programme.title: Anchorman 2: The Legend Continues
programme.desc: Ron Burgundy tries to stay classy when he and his team take New York and the nation's first 24-hour global cable news network by storm.

programme.title: Roofman
programme.desc: Escaped convict Jeffrey Manchester hides out in a Toys "R" Us, surviving undetected for months while planning his next move. However, his double life starts to unravel when he falls for a divorced mum, setting off a compelling game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: Seven Psychopaths

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 2

programme.title: Yesterday
programme.desc: Jack Malik is a struggling singer-songwriter in an English seaside town whose dreams of fame are rapidly fading, despite the fierce devotion and support of his childhood best friend, Ellie. After a freak bus accident during a mysterious global blackout, Jack wakes up to discover that The Beatles have never existed. Performing songs by the greatest band in history to a world that has never heard them, Jack becomes on overnight sensation with a little help from his agent.

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle
programme.desc: A recently engaged woman becomes obsessed with meeting the grieving widower she heard on a late night radio show. Sam, the widower, receives hundreds of letters following the radio phone-in which was instigated by his son Jonah.

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 3
programme.desc: After the highs of winning the world championships, the Bellas find themselves split apart. But when they get the chance to reunite for an overseas USO tour, this group of awesome nerds will come together to make some music one last time.

programme.title: Mean Machine
programme.desc: A footballer is jailed for throwing a crucial game. Whilst inside, the football-loving warden sets him the task of coaching a team of prisoners to take on the all-conquering warders' side. Can he form a disparate bunch of prisoners into a winning team?

programme.title: Mamma Mia!
programme.desc: Set on a Greek island, a young woman who is about to be married suspects that any one of three men could be her father. She invites them all to her wedding without telling her mother. Movie adaptation of the musical, set to Abba's greatest hits.

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again
programme.desc: In 1979 Donna falls in love with Sam while embarking on a series of adventures throughout Europe. In the present day, Donna's pregnant daughter reunites with her mother's old friends and boyfriends on the Greek island of Kalokairi.

programme.desc: In 1991, talented figure skater Tonya Harding becomes the first American woman to complete a triple axel during a competition. In 1994, her world comes crashing down when her ex-husband conspires to injure Nancy Kerrigan, a fellow Olympic hopeful, in a poorly conceived attack that forces the young woman to withdraw from the national championship. Harding's life and legacy instantly become tarnished as she's forever associated with one of the most infamous scandals in sports history.

programme.title: Erin Brockovich
programme.desc: Based on the true story of an unsophisticated single mother who stumbles upon an environmental cover-up. While working for a lawyer, she is puzzled by an old case and decides to do some research of her own. She discovers that a chemical company knowingly polluted the local area, and with the help of the lawyer launches a class action to sue the company for damages.

programme.title: The Bodyguard
programme.desc: A Secret Service agent-turned-bodyguard finds danger and romance while protecting an entertainer from a stalker.
